title: The Download: the internet’s best weather app, and why people freeze their brains
summary: OpenSnow, a startup weather forecasting app, has become a leading tool for ski condition predictions by leveraging government data and proprietary AI models rather than traditional federally-funded services. The app demonstrates how specialized, AI-enhanced weather forecasting can outperform established alternatives in specific domains.
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How a couple of ski bums built the internet’s best weather app  The best snow-forecasting app for skiers isn’t a federally-funded service or a big-name brand. It’s OpenSnow, a startup that uses government data, its own AI…

zh.title: 下载：互联网最好的天气应用程序，以及人们为什么冻结他们的大脑
zh.summary: OpenSnow是一个初创天气预报应用程序，通过利用政府数据和专有AI模型而不是传统的联邦资助服务，已成为滑雪条件预测的领先工具。该应用程序展示了专业的、AI增强的天气预报如何能够在特定领域中优于已建立的替代方案。

fr.title: Le Téléchargement : la meilleure application météo d'Internet et pourquoi les gens gèlent leur cerveau
fr.summary: OpenSnow, une application de prévisions météorologiques en démarrage, est devenue un outil de premier plan pour les prédictions des conditions de ski en exploitant les données gouvernementales et les modèles d'IA propriétaires plutôt que les services traditionnellement financés par le gouvernement fédéral. L'application démontre comment les prévisions météorologiques spécialisées et améliorées par l'IA peuvent surpasser les alternatives établies dans des domaines spécifiques.

de.title: Der Download: die beste Wetter-App des Internets und warum Menschen ihr Gehirn einfrieren
de.summary: OpenSnow, eine Startup-Wetter-Vorhersage-App, ist durch die Nutzung von Regierungsdaten und proprietären KI-Modellen anstelle traditioneller bundesfinanzierter Dienste zu einem führenden Werkzeug für Vorhersagen von Skibedingungen geworden. Die App zeigt, wie spezialisierte, KI-verbesserte Wettervorhersagen etablierte Alternativen in bestimmten Bereichen übertreffen können.

es.title: La Descarga: la mejor aplicación meteorológica de Internet y por qué las personas congelan sus cerebros
es.summary: OpenSnow, una aplicación de pronósticos meteorológicos de inicio, se ha convertido en una herramienta líder para predicciones de condiciones de esquí al aprovechar datos gubernamentales y modelos de IA propietarios en lugar de servicios financiados tradicionalmente por el gobierno federal. La aplicación demuestra cómo los pronósticos meteorológicos especializados y mejorados por IA pueden superar las alternativas establecidas en dominios específicos.

title: Here’s why some people choose cryonics to store their bodies and brains after death
summary: The article explores why individuals opt for cryonics to preserve their bodies and brains after death, using the example of L. Stephen Coles, a gerontologist who specialized in human longevity and chose cryonic preservation. This reflects broader interest in life-extension technologies and their ethical and scientific implications.
content: This week I reported on some rather unusual research that focuses on the brain of L. Stephen Coles. Coles was a gerontologist who died from pancreatic cancer in 2014. He had spent the latter part of his career specializing in human longevity. And before he died, he decided to have his brain preserved by a…

zh.title: 以下是人们选择冷冻保存他们的身体和大脑的原因
zh.summary: 该文章通过人口老龄学专家L·斯蒂芬·科尔斯的例子，探讨了为什么人们选择冷冻保存来保存他们的身体和大脑，他专门研究人类寿命并选择了冷冻保存。这反映了人们对生命延长技术及其伦理和科学影响的广泛兴趣。

fr.title: Voici pourquoi certaines personnes choisissent la cryonie pour conserver leur corps et leur cerveau après la mort
fr.summary: L'article explore pourquoi les individus optent pour la cryonie pour préserver leurs corps et leurs cerveaux après la mort, en utilisant l'exemple de L. Stephen Coles, un gérontologue spécialisé dans la longévité humaine qui a choisi la préservation cryonique. Cela reflète un intérêt plus large pour les technologies d'extension de la vie et leurs implications éthiques et scientifiques.

de.title: Hier ist der Grund, warum sich manche Menschen für Kryonik entscheiden, um ihre Körper und Gehirne nach dem Tod zu konservieren
de.summary: Der Artikel untersucht, warum sich Einzelpersonen für Kryonik entscheiden, um ihre Körper und Gehirne nach dem Tod zu konservieren, unter Verwendung des Beispiels von L. Stephen Coles, einem Gerontologen, der sich auf menschliche Langlebigkeit spezialisiert hat und sich für kryonische Konservierung entschied. Dies spiegelt ein breiteres Interesse an Lebensverlängerungstechnologien und deren ethischen und wissenschaftlichen Auswirkungen wider.

es.title: Aquí está por qué algunas personas eligen la criogenia para conservar sus cuerpos y cerebros después de la muerte
es.summary: El artículo explora por qué los individuos optan por la criogenia para preservar sus cuerpos y cerebros después de la muerte, utilizando el ejemplo de L. Stephen Coles, un gerontólogo que se especializó en longevidad humana y eligió la preservación criogénica. Esto refleja un interés más amplio en tecnologías de extensión de la vida y sus implicaciones éticas y científicas.

title: ARC-AGI-3: A New Challenge for Frontier Agentic Intelligence
summary: ARC-AGI-3 introduces a benchmark for evaluating agentic intelligence on novel, abstract tasks without language or external knowledge, with humans achieving 100% accuracy while frontier AI systems score below 1%. The benchmark uses difficulty-calibrated environments and efficiency-based scoring to assess fluid adaptation to novel problems.
content:
arXiv:2603.24621v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce ARC-AGI-3, an interactive benchmark for studying agentic intelligence through novel, abstract, turn-based environments in which agents must explore, infer goals, build internal models of environment dynamics, and plan effective action sequences without explicit instructions. Like its predecessors ARC-AGI-1 and 2, ARC-AGI-3 focuses entirely on evaluating fluid adaptive efficiency on novel tasks, while avoiding language and external knowledge. ARC-AGI-3 environments only leverage Core Knowledge priors and are difficulty-calibrated via extensive testing with human test-takers. Our testing shows humans can solve 100% of the environments, in contrast to frontier AI systems which, as of March 2026, score below 1%. In this paper, we present the benchmark design, its efficiency-based scoring framework grounded in human action baselines, and the methodology used to construct, validate, and calibrate the environments.

zh.title: ARC-AGI-3：前沿代理智能的新挑战
zh.summary: ARC-AGI-3引入了一个基准，用于评估代理智能在无需语言或外部知识的新颖抽象任务上的表现，人类实现100%的准确率，而前沿AI系统得分不到1%。该基准使用难度校准的环境和基于效率的评分来评估对新问题的流利适应能力。

fr.title: ARC-AGI-3 : Un nouveau défi pour l'intelligence agentive de frontier
fr.summary: ARC-AGI-3 introduit un benchmark pour évaluer l'intelligence agentive sur des tâches abstraites nouvelles sans langage ni connaissance externe, les humains atteignant 100% de précision tandis que les systèmes d'IA de frontier obtiennent un score inférieur à 1%. Le benchmark utilise des environnements étalonnés en difficulté et un score basé sur l'efficacité pour évaluer l'adaptation fluide à de nouveaux problèmes.

de.title: ARC-AGI-3: Eine neue Herausforderung für Frontier-Agentic-Intelligenz
de.summary: ARC-AGI-3 führt einen Benchmark zur Bewertung der Agentic Intelligence bei neuartigen abstrakten Aufgaben ohne Sprache oder externes Wissen ein, wobei Menschen eine Genauigkeit von 100% erreichen, während frontier-AI-Systeme unter 1% erzielen. Der Benchmark verwendet schwierigkeitskalibrierte Umgebungen und effizenzbasierte Bewertungen, um die flüssige Anpassung an neue Probleme zu bewerten.

es.title: ARC-AGI-3: Un nuevo desafío para la inteligencia agentiva fronteriza
es.summary: ARC-AGI-3 introduce un punto de referencia para evaluar la inteligencia agentiva en tareas abstractas novedosas sin lenguaje ni conocimiento externo, con humanos logrando 100% de precisión mientras que los sistemas de IA fronterizos puntúan por debajo del 1%. El punto de referencia utiliza entornos calibrados por dificultad y puntuación basada en la eficiencia para evaluar la adaptación fluida a nuevos problemas.

title: When Is Collective Intelligence a Lottery? Multi-Agent Scaling Laws for Memetic Drift in LLMs
summary: Multi-agent LLM systems reach consensus through 'memetic drift'—where arbitrary sampling choices compound into agreement—rather than through collective reasoning. The research establishes scaling laws distinguishing drift-dominated regimes (lottery-like outcomes) from selection regimes where weak biases amplify.
content:
arXiv:2603.24676v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multi-agent systems powered by large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in settings that shape consequential decisions, both directly and indirectly. Yet it remains unclear whether their outcomes reflect collective reasoning, systematic bias, or mere chance. Recent work has sharpened this question with naming games, showing that even when no individual agent favors any label a priori, populations rapidly break symmetry and reach consensus. Here, we reveal the mechanism by introducing a minimal model, Quantized Simplex Gossip (QSG), and trace the microscopic origin of this agreement to mutual in-context learning. In QSG, agents maintain internal belief states but learn from one another's sampled outputs, so one agent's arbitrary choice becomes the next agent's evidence and can compound toward agreement. By analogy with neutral evolution, we call this sampling-driven regime memetic drift. QSG predicts a crossover from a drift-dominated regime, where consensus is effectively a lottery, to a selection regime, where weak biases are amplified and shape the outcome. We derive scaling laws for drift-induced polarization as a function of population size, communication bandwidth, in-context adaptation rate, and agents' internal uncertainty, and we validate them in both QSG simulations and naming-game experiments with LLM populations. Together, these results provide a framework for studying the collective mechanisms of social representation formation in multi-agent systems.

zh.title: 集体智能何时是彩票？LLM中多智能体缩放法则的模因漂移
zh.summary: 多智能体LLM系统通过"模因漂移"达成共识，其中任意的采样选择复合成一致，而不是通过集体推理。该研究建立了缩放法则，区分漂移主导的体制（彩票般的结果）和弱偏见放大的选择体制。

fr.title: Quand l'intelligence collective est-elle une loterie ? Lois d'échelle multi-agents pour la dérive mémétique dans les LLM
fr.summary: Les systèmes multi-agents LLM parviennent à un consensus par 'dérive mémétique'—où les choix d'échantillonnage arbitraires se composent en accord—plutôt que par raisonnement collectif. La recherche établit des lois d'échelle distinguant les régimes dominés par la dérive (résultats type loterie) des régimes de sélection où les biais faibles s'amplifient.

de.title: Wann ist kollektive Intelligenz eine Lotterie? Multi-Agent-Skalierungsgesetze für memetischen Drift in LLMs
de.summary: Multi-Agent-LLM-Systeme erreichen Konsens durch 'memetischen Drift'—wobei willkürliche Stichprobenwahlentscheidungen zu Übereinstimmung führen—anstatt durch kollektive Argumentation. Die Forschung etabliert Skalierungsgesetze, die drift-dominierte Regimen (lotterieähnliche Ergebnisse) von Auswahlregimen unterscheiden, wo schwache Verzerrungen verstärkt werden.

es.title: ¿Cuándo es la inteligencia colectiva una lotería? Leyes de escala multi-agente para la deriva memética en LLM
es.summary: Los sistemas de múltiples agentes LLM llegan a consenso a través de 'deriva memética'—donde las opciones de muestreo arbitrarias se componen en acuerdo—en lugar de a través del razonamiento colectivo. La investigación establece leyes de escala que distinguen regímenes dominados por la deriva (resultados tipo lotería) de regímenes de selección donde los sesgos débiles se amplifican.

title: AutoSAM: an Agentic Framework for Automating Input File Generation for the SAM Code with Multi-Modal Retrieval-Augmented Generation
summary: AutoSAM automates reactor simulation input generation from engineering documents using LLM agents with retrieval-augmented generation, ingesting unstructured data to produce validated solver models. The framework achieves ~88% extraction from PDFs and 100% from visual content while identifying missing data transparently.
content:
arXiv:2603.24736v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In the design and safety analysis of advanced reactor systems, constructing input files for system-level thermal-hydraulics codes such as the System Analysis Module (SAM) remains a labor-intensive task. Analysts must extract and reconcile design data from heterogeneous engineering documents and manually translate it into solver-specific syntax. In this paper, we present AutoSAM, an agentic framework that automates SAM input file generation. The framework combines a large language model agent with retrieval-augmented generation over the solver's user guide and theory manual, together with specialized tools for analyzing PDFs, images, spreadsheets, and text files. AutoSAM ingests unstructured engineering documents, including system diagrams, design reports, and data tables, extracts simulation-relevant parameters into a human-auditable intermediate representation, and synthesizes validated, solver-compatible input decks. Its multimodal retrieval pipeline integrates scientific text extraction, vision-based figure interpretation, semantic embedding, and query answering. We evaluate AutoSAM on four case studies of increasing complexity: a single-pipe steady-state model, a solid-fuel channel with temperature reactivity feedback, the Advanced Burner Test Reactor core, and the Molten Salt Reactor Experiment primary loop. Across all cases, the agent produces runnable SAM models consistent with expected thermal-hydraulic behavior while explicitly identifying missing data and labeling assumed values. The framework achieves 100% utilization of structured inputs, about 88% extraction from PDF text, and 100% completeness in vision-based geometric extraction. These results demonstrate a practical path toward prompt-driven reactor modeling, in which analysts provide system descriptions and supporting documentation while the agent translates them into transparent, and executable, SAM simulations.

zh.title: AutoSAM：使用多模态检索增强生成自动化SAM代码输入文件生成的代理框架
zh.summary: AutoSAM使用带有检索增强生成的LLM代理从工程文档自动化反应堆仿真输入生成，摄取非结构化数据以产生经过验证的求解器模型。该框架从PDF中实现约88%的提取率，从视觉内容中实现100%的提取率，同时透明地识别缺失数据。

fr.title: AutoSAM : un cadre agentique pour automatiser la génération de fichiers d'entrée pour le code SAM avec génération augmentée par récupération multimodale
fr.summary: AutoSAM automatise la génération d'entrée de simulation de réacteur à partir de documents d'ingénierie en utilisant des agents LLM avec génération augmentée par récupération, ingérant des données non structurées pour produire des modèles de solveur validés. Le cadre réalise ~88% d'extraction à partir de PDF et 100% à partir de contenu visuel tout en identifiant de manière transparente les données manquantes.

de.title: AutoSAM: Ein agentic-Framework zur Automatisierung der Eingabedateigenerierung für SAM-Code mit Multi-Modal Retrieval-Augmented Generation
de.summary: AutoSAM automatisiert die Eingabegenerierung von Reaktorsimulationen aus Ingenieurdokumenten unter Verwendung von LLM-Agenten mit Retrieval-Augmented Generation, nimmt unstrukturierte Daten auf, um validierte Solver-Modelle zu erstellen. Das Framework erreicht ~88% Extraktion aus PDFs und 100% aus visuellem Inhalt, während es fehlende Daten transparent identifiziert.

es.title: AutoSAM: un marco agentivo para automatizar la generación de archivos de entrada para código SAM con generación aumentada por recuperación multimodal
es.summary: AutoSAM automatiza la generación de entrada de simulación de reactores a partir de documentos de ingeniería utilizando agentes LLM con generación aumentada por recuperación, ingiriendo datos no estructurados para producir modelos de solucionador validados. El marco logra aproximadamente 88% de extracción de archivos PDF y 100% de contenido visual mientras identifica transparentemente datos faltantes.

title: Trust as Monitoring: Evolutionary Dynamics of User Trust and AI Developer Behaviour
summary: An evolutionary game theory model demonstrates that user trust functions as reduced monitoring of AI developers, identifying three equilibrium outcomes ranging from unsafe development without adoption to safe systems with widespread adoption. Achieving safe deployment requires governance emphasizing transparency, low-cost monitoring, and meaningful penalties.
content:
arXiv:2603.24742v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI safety is an increasingly urgent concern as the capabilities and adoption of AI systems grow. Existing evolutionary models of AI governance have primarily examined incentives for safe development and effective regulation, typically representing users' trust as a one-shot adoption choice rather than as a dynamic, evolving process shaped by repeated interactions. We instead model trust as reduced monitoring in a repeated, asymmetric interaction between users and AI developers, where checking AI behaviour is costly. Using evolutionary game theory, we study how user trust strategies and developer choices between safe (compliant) and unsafe (non-compliant) AI co-evolve under different levels of monitoring cost and institutional regimes. We complement the infinite-population replicator analysis with stochastic finite-population dynamics and reinforcement learning (Q-learning) simulations. Across these approaches, we find three robust long-run regimes: no adoption with unsafe development, unsafe but widely adopted systems, and safe systems that are widely adopted. Only the last is desirable, and it arises when penalties for unsafe behaviour exceed the extra cost of safety and users can still afford to monitor at least occasionally. Our results formally support governance proposals that emphasise transparency, low-cost monitoring, and meaningful sanctions, and they show that neither regulation alone nor blind user trust is sufficient to prevent evolutionary drift towards unsafe or low-adoption outcomes.

zh.title: 信任作为监控：用户信任和AI开发者行为的演化动力学
zh.summary: 进化博弈论模型表明，用户信任充当对AI开发者的监控减少，确定了三种均衡结果，范围从没有采用的不安全开发到广泛采用的安全系统。实现安全部署需要强调透明度、低成本监控和有意义的处罚的治理。

fr.title: La confiance comme surveillance : dynamique évolutive de la confiance des utilisateurs et du comportement des développeurs d'IA
fr.summary: Un modèle de théorie des jeux évolutifs démontre que la confiance des utilisateurs fonctionne comme une surveillance réduite des développeurs d'IA, identifiant trois résultats d'équilibre allant du développement non sûr sans adoption aux systèmes sûrs avec adoption généralisée. Atteindre un déploiement sûr nécessite une gouvernance mettant l'accent sur la transparence, la surveillance à faible coût et des pénalités significatives.

de.title: Vertrauen als Überwachung: Evolutionäre Dynamik des Benutzervertrauens und des Verhaltens von KI-Entwicklern
de.summary: Ein evolutionäres Spieltheorie-Modell zeigt, dass Benutzervertrauen als reduzierte Überwachung von KI-Entwicklern funktioniert und identifiziert drei Gleichgewichtsergebnisse, die von unsicherer Entwicklung ohne Annahme bis zu sicheren Systemen mit weit verbreiteter Annahme reichen. Die Erreichung einer sicheren Bereitstellung erfordert eine Governance, die Transparenz, kostengünstige Überwachung und aussagekräftige Strafen betont.

es.title: La confianza como monitoreo: dinámicas evolutivas de la confianza del usuario y el comportamiento del desarrollador de IA
es.summary: Un modelo de teoría de juegos evolutiva demuestra que la confianza del usuario funciona como una supervisión reducida de los desarrolladores de IA, identificando tres resultados de equilibrio que van desde el desarrollo inseguro sin adopción hasta sistemas seguros con adopción generalizada. Lograr una implementación segura requiere una gobernanza que enfatice la transparencia, el monitoreo de bajo costo y sanciones significativas.

title: Formal Semantics for Agentic Tool Protocols: A Process Calculus Approach
summary: The first formal process calculus proves Schema-Guided Dialogue (SGD) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) are structurally bisimilar while revealing critical expressivity gaps in MCP. Five additional principles are identified as necessary for full behavioral equivalence between the frameworks.
content:
arXiv:2603.24747v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The emergence of large language model agents capable of invoking external tools has created urgent need for formal verification of agent protocols. Two paradigms dominate this space: Schema-Guided Dialogue (SGD), a research framework for zero-shot API generalization, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an industry standard for agent-tool integration. While both enable dynamic service discovery through schema descriptions, their formal relationship remains unexplored. Building on prior work establishing the conceptual convergence of these paradigms, we present the first process calculus formalization of SGD and MCP, proving they are structurally bisimilar under a well-defined mapping Phi. However, we demonstrate that the reverse mapping Phi^{-1} is partial and lossy, revealing critical gaps in MCP's expressivity. Through bidirectional analysis, we identify five principles -- semantic completeness, explicit action boundaries, failure mode documentation, progressive disclosure compatibility, and inter-tool relationship declaration -- as necessary and sufficient conditions for full behavioral equivalence. We formalize these principles as type-system extensions MCP+, proving MCP+ is isomorphic to SGD. Our work provides the first formal foundation for verified agent systems and establishes schema quality as a provable safety property.

zh.title: 智能代理工具协议的形式语义：过程演算方法
zh.summary: 第一个形式过程演算证明了模式导向对话（SGD）和模型上下文协议（MCP）在结构上是互相模拟的，同时揭示了MCP中的关键表达能力差距。识别了五个额外的原则，这些原则对于框架之间的完全行为等价性是必要的。

fr.title: Sémantique formelle pour les protocoles d'outils d'agents : une approche de calcul de processus
fr.summary: Le premier calcul de processus formel prouve que le dialogue guidé par schéma (SGD) et le protocole de contexte modèle (MCP) sont structurellement bisimilaires tout en révélant des lacunes critiques d'expressivité dans MCP. Cinq principes supplémentaires sont identifiés comme nécessaires pour une équivalence comportementale complète entre les cadres.

de.title: Formale Semantik für Agentic Tool Protokolle: Ein Prozesskalküls-Ansatz
de.summary: Der erste formale Prozesskalkül beweist, dass schemagesteuerte Dialoge (SGD) und das Modellkontextprotokoll (MCP) strukturell bisimilar sind, während er kritische Ausdruckslücken in MCP offenbart. Fünf zusätzliche Prinzipien werden als notwendig für vollständige Verhaltensäquivalenz zwischen den Rahmenwerken identifiziert.

es.title: Semántica formal para protocolos de herramientas de agentes: un enfoque de cálculo de procesos
es.summary: El primer cálculo de procesos formal demuestra que el diálogo guiado por esquemas (SGD) y el protocolo de contexto modelo (MCP) son estructuralmente bisimilares mientras revela brechas críticas de expresividad en MCP. Se identifican cinco principios adicionales como necesarios para la equivalencia de comportamiento completa entre los marcos.

title: Supervising Ralph Wiggum: Exploring a Metacognitive Co-Regulation Agentic AI Loop for Engineering Design
summary: A Co-Regulation Design Agentic Loop helps engineering design agents overcome fixation by using a metacognitive supervisor to encourage exploration of alternative solutions. Testing shows the system generates superior performance without significant computational overhead compared to self-regulating baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.24768v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The engineering design research community has studied agentic AI systems that use Large Language Model (LLM) agents to automate the engineering design process. However, these systems are prone to some of the same pathologies that plague humans. Just as human designers, LLM design agents can fixate on existing paradigms and fail to explore alternatives when solving design challenges, potentially leading to suboptimal solutions. In this work, we propose (1) a novel Self-Regulation Loop (SRL), in which the Design Agent self-regulates and explicitly monitors its own metacognition, and (2) a novel Co-Regulation Design Agentic Loop (CRDAL), in which a Metacognitive Co-Regulation Agent assists the Design Agent in metacognition to mitigate design fixation, thereby improving system performance for engineering design tasks. In the battery pack design problem examined here, we found that the novel CRDAL system generates designs with better performance, without significantly increasing the computational cost, compared to a plain Ralph Wiggum Loop (RWL) and the metacognitively self-assessing Self-Regulation Loop (SRL). Also, we found that the CRDAL system navigated through the latent design space more effectively than both SRL and RWL. However, the SRL did not generate designs with significantly better performance than RWL, even though it explored a different region of the design space. The proposed system architectures and findings of this work provide practical implications for future development of agentic AI systems for engineering design.

zh.title: 监督Ralph Wiggum：探索工程设计的元认知共同调节代理AI循环
zh.summary: 协同调节设计代理循环通过使用元认知监督者鼓励探索替代解决方案，帮助工程设计代理克服固定性。测试显示该系统产生优越的性能，与自我调节基准相比计算开销不显著。

fr.title: Superviser Ralph Wiggum : explorer une boucle d'IA agentique de corégulation métacognitive pour la conception d'ingénierie
fr.summary: Une boucle de conception agentique de corégulation aide les agents de conception d'ingénierie à surmonter la fixation en utilisant un superviseur métacognitif pour encourager l'exploration de solutions alternatives. Les tests montrent que le système génère des performances supérieures sans surcharge de calcul significative par rapport aux références d'autorégulation.

de.title: Ralph Wiggum beaufsichtigen: Erkundung einer metakognitiven Co-Regulations-Agentic-AI-Schleife für das Engineering-Design
de.summary: Eine Co-Regulations-Design-Agentic-Schleife hilft Engineering-Design-Agenten, Festlegungen zu überwinden, indem ein metakognitiver Supervisor die Erkundung alternativer Lösungen fördert. Tests zeigen, dass das System überlegene Leistung ohne signifikanten Rechenaufwand im Vergleich zu selbstregulierenden Baselines erzeugt.

es.title: Supervisando a Ralph Wiggum: Explorando un bucle de IA agentivo de corregulación metacognitiva para diseño de ingeniería
es.summary: Un bucle de diseño agentivo de corregulación ayuda a los agentes de diseño de ingeniería a superar la fijación utilizando un supervisor metacognitivo para fomentar la exploración de soluciones alternativas. Las pruebas muestran que el sistema genera un desempeño superior sin sobrecarga computacional significativa en comparación con los basales autorregulados.

title: ReLope: KL-Regularized LoRA Probes for Multimodal LLM Routing
summary: ReLope introduces KL-regularized LoRA probes to improve correctness prediction in multimodal LLMs for intelligent model routing, addressing signal degradation caused by visual inputs. The methods consistently outperform baselines by improving hidden state quality for routing decisions.
content:
arXiv:2603.24787v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Routing has emerged as a promising strategy for balancing performance and cost in large language model (LLM) systems that combine lightweight models with powerful but expensive large models. Recent studies show that \emph{probe routing}, which predicts the correctness of a small model using its hidden states, provides an effective solution in text-only LLMs. However, we observe that these probes degrade substantially when applied to multimodal LLMs (MLLMs). Through empirical analysis, we find that the presence of visual inputs weakens the separability of correctness signals in hidden states, making them harder to extract using standard probe designs. To address this challenge, we introduce two complementary approaches for improving probe routing in MLLMs. First, we propose the \emph{Attention Probe}, which aggregates hidden states from the preceding layer based on attention scores to recover distributed correctness signals. Second, we present the \emph{KL-Regularized LoRA Probe (ReLope)}, which inserts a lightweight LoRA adapter and applies a KL regularizer to learn routing-aware representations. Comprehensive experiments show that our methods consistently outperform baselines, suggesting that improving the quality of hidden states is key to effective routing in MLLMs. Our code is available at https://github.com/Spinozaaa/ReLope.

zh.title: ReLope: 用于多模态LLM路由的KL正则化LoRA探针
zh.summary: ReLope引入KL正则化LoRA探针，通过改进隐藏状态质量来提高多模态LLM中的正确性预测，用于智能模型路由，解决视觉输入造成的信号衰减问题。该方法在改进路由决策的隐藏状态质量方面持续优于基准。

fr.title: ReLope: Sondes LoRA régularisées par KL pour le routage LLM multimodal
fr.summary: ReLope introduit des sondes LoRA régularisées par KL pour améliorer la prédiction de exactitude dans les LLM multimodaux pour le routage intelligent des modèles, en résolvant la dégradation du signal causée par les entrées visuelles. Les méthodes surpassent constamment les valeurs de base en améliorant la qualité de l'état caché pour les décisions de routage.

de.title: ReLope: KL-regularisierte LoRA-Sonden für Multimodal-LLM-Routing
de.summary: ReLope führt KL-regularisierte LoRA-Sonden ein, um die Korrektheitsprognose in Multimodal-LLMs für intelligentes Modell-Routing zu verbessern, wobei die durch visuelle Eingaben verursachte Signaldegradation behoben wird. Die Methoden übertreffen die Baselines konstant durch Verbesserung der verborgenen Zustandsqualität für Routing-Entscheidungen.

es.title: ReLope: Sondas LoRA regularizadas por KL para el enrutamiento de LLM multimodal
es.summary: ReLope introduce sondas LoRA regularizadas por KL para mejorar la predicción de exactitud en LLM multimodales para enrutamiento inteligente de modelos, abordando la degradación de señal causada por entradas visuales. Los métodos consistentemente superan los valores base mejorando la calidad del estado oculto para decisiones de enrutamiento.

title: Resisting Humanization: Ethical Front-End Design Choices in AI for Sensitive Contexts
summary: Humanizing design elements in conversational AI—dialogue, personality modes, emotive language—profoundly shape user trust and expectations, risking misplaced confidence in vulnerable populations. The work argues for principled ethical restraint in interface design rather than engagement-driven norms, demonstrated through trauma-informed design case studies.
content:
arXiv:2603.24853v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Ethical debates in AI have primarily focused on back-end issues such as data governance, model training, and algorithmic decision-making. Less attention has been paid to the ethical significance of front-end design choices, such as the interaction and representation-based elements through which users interact with AI systems. This gap is particularly significant for Conversational User Interfaces (CUI) based on Natural Language Processing (NLP) systems, where humanizing design elements such as dialogue-based interaction, emotive language, personality modes, and anthropomorphic metaphors are increasingly prevalent. This work argues that humanization in AI front-end design is a value-driven choice that profoundly shapes users' mental models, trust calibration, and behavioral responses. Drawing on research in human-computer interaction (HCI), conversational AI, and value-sensitive design, we examine how interfaces can play a central role in misaligning user expectations, fostering misplaced trust, and subtly undermining user autonomy, especially in vulnerable contexts. To ground this analysis, we discuss two AI systems developed by Chayn, a nonprofit organization supporting survivors of gender-based violence. Chayn is extremely cautious when building AI that interacts with or impacts survivors by operationalizing their trauma-informed design principles. This Chayn case study illustrates how ethical considerations can motivate principled restraint in interface design, challenging engagement-based norms in contemporary AI products. We argue that ethical front-end AI design is a form of procedural ethics, enacted through interaction choices rather than embedded solely in system logic.

zh.title: 抵制人性化：AI在敏感环境中的伦理前端设计选择
zh.summary: 对话AI中的人性化设计元素——对话、性格模式、情感语言——深刻影响用户信任和期望，对脆弱群体造成不当信心的风险。该工作主张在接口设计中采取有原则的伦理克制，而非参与驱动规范，通过创伤知情设计案例研究来演示。

fr.title: Résister à l'humanisation: Choix de conception éthiques d'interface pour l'IA dans les contextes sensibles
fr.summary: Les éléments de conception humanisants dans l'IA conversationnelle—dialogue, modes de personnalité, langage émotif—façonnent profondément la confiance et les attentes des utilisateurs, risquant une confiance mal placée dans les populations vulnérables. Le travail défend la retenue éthique rationnelle dans la conception d'interface plutôt que les normes axées sur l'engagement, démontré par des études de cas de conception informée par le traumatisme.

de.title: Humanisierung widerstehen: Ethische Front-End-Design-Entscheidungen in KI für sensible Kontexte
de.summary: Humanisierende Designelemente in Conversational AI – Dialog, Persönlichkeitsmodi, emotionale Sprache – prägen tiefgreifend Nutzervertrauen und Erwartungen und riskieren unberechtigtes Vertrauen bei anfälligen Bevölkerungsgruppen. Die Arbeit argumentiert für prinzipierte ethische Zurückhaltung im Interface-Design anstelle von Engagement-getriebenen Normen, demonstriert durch traumainformierte Design-Fallstudien.

es.title: Resistir la humanización: Opciones de diseño ético de interfaz en IA para contextos sensibles
es.summary: Los elementos de diseño humanizantes en IA conversacional—diálogo, modos de personalidad, lenguaje emotivo—moldean profundamente la confianza y las expectativas del usuario, arriesgando confianza mal colocada en poblaciones vulnerables. El trabajo aboga por desistimiento ético principiado en el diseño de interfaz en lugar de normas impulsadas por el compromiso, demostrado a través de estudios de caso de diseño informado por el trauma.

title: SentinelAI: A Multi-Agent Framework for Structuring and Linking NG9-1-1 Emergency Incident Data
summary: SentinelAI automates integration of fragmented emergency communications into standardized NG9-1-1 datasets through specialized agents, enabling unified incident views and real-time cross-source reasoning. The framework produces machine-readable incident objects from raw communications across multiple agencies.
content:
arXiv:2603.24856v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Emergency response systems generate data from many agencies and systems. In practice, correlating and updating this information across sources in a way that aligns with Next Generation 9-1-1 data standards remains challenging. Ideally, this data should be treated as a continuous stream of operational updates, where new facts are integrated immediately to provide a timely and unified view of an evolving incident. This paper presents SentinelAI, a data integration and standardization framework for transforming emergency communications into standardized, machine-readable datasets that support integration, composite incident construction, and cross-source reasoning. SentinelAI implements a scalable processing pipeline composed of specialized agents. The EIDO Agent ingests raw communications and produces NENA-compliant Emergency Incident Data Object JSON.

zh.title: SentinelAI：用于构建和链接NG9-1-1紧急事件数据的多智能体框架
zh.summary: SentinelAI通过专门的智能体自动化将分散的应急通信集成到标准化的NG9-1-1数据集中，实现统一的事件视图和实时跨源推理。该框架从多个机构的原始通信中生成机器可读的事件对象。

fr.title: SentinelAI: Un cadre multi-agents pour la structuration et la liaison des données d'incidents d'urgence NG9-1-1
fr.summary: SentinelAI automatise l'intégration des communications d'urgence fragmentées dans des ensembles de données NG9-1-1 standardisés par le biais d'agents spécialisés, permettant des vues d'incidents unifiées et des raisonnements cross-source en temps réel. Le cadre produit des objets d'incidents lisibles par machine à partir des communications brutes provenant de plusieurs agences.

de.title: SentinelAI: Ein Multi-Agent-Framework zur Strukturierung und Verknüpfung von NG9-1-1-Notfallincident-Daten
de.summary: SentinelAI automatisiert die Integration fragmentierter Notfallkommunikationen in standardisierte NG9-1-1-Datensätze durch spezialisierte Agenten, was einheitliche Incident-Ansichten und echtzeit-übergreifendes Schließen ermöglicht. Das Framework produziert maschinenlebare Incident-Objekte aus unverarbeiteter Kommunikation über mehrere Behörden hinweg.

es.title: SentinelAI: Un marco multi-agente para estructurar y vincular datos de incidentes de emergencia NG9-1-1
es.summary: SentinelAI automatiza la integración de comunicaciones de emergencia fragmentadas en conjuntos de datos NG9-1-1 estandarizados a través de agentes especializados, permitiendo vistas de incidentes unificadas y razonamiento en tiempo real entre fuentes. El marco produce objetos de incidentes legibles por máquina a partir de comunicaciones brutas de múltiples agencias.

title: How Far Are Vision-Language Models from Constructing the Real World? A Benchmark for Physical Generative Reasoning
summary: DreamHouse benchmark tests vision-language models' physical generative reasoning—synthesizing artifacts that satisfy structural and code-compliance constraints—rather than visual plausibility, using 26,000 verified timber-frame structures. Experiments reveal substantial capability gaps invisible in visual realism metrics.
content:
arXiv:2603.24866v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The physical world is not merely visual; it is governed by rigorous structural and procedural constraints. Yet, the evaluation of vision-language models (VLMs) remains heavily skewed toward perceptual realism, prioritizing the generation of visually plausible 3D layouts, shapes, and appearances. Current benchmarks rarely test whether models grasp the step-by-step processes and physical dependencies required to actually build these artifacts, a capability essential for automating design-to-construction pipelines. To address this, we introduce DreamHouse, a novel benchmark for physical generative reasoning: the capacity to synthesize artifacts that concurrently satisfy geometric, structural, constructability, and code-compliance constraints. We ground this benchmark in residential timber-frame construction, a domain with fully codified engineering standards and objectively verifiable correctness. We curate over 26,000 structures spanning 13 architectural styles, ach verified to construction-document standards (LOD 350) and develop a deterministic 10-test structural validation framework. Unlike static benchmarks that assess only final outputs, DreamHouse supports iterative agentic interaction. Models observe intermediate build states, generate construction actions, and receive structured environmental feedback, enabling a fine-grained evaluation of planning, structural reasoning, and self-correction. Extensive experiments with state-of-the-art VLMs reveal substantial capability gaps that are largely invisible on existing leaderboards. These findings establish physical validity as a critical evaluation axis orthogonal to visual realism, highlighting physical generative reasoning as a distinct and underdeveloped frontier in multimodal intelligence. Available at https://luluyuyuyang.github.io/dreamhouse

zh.title: 视觉语言模型距离构建真实世界有多远？物理生成推理基准
zh.summary: DreamHouse基准测试视觉语言模型的物理生成推理能力——合成满足结构和代码合规约束的工件，而非视觉逼真度——使用26,000个经过验证的木框架结构。实验揭示了在视觉真实性指标中不可见的实质性能力差距。

fr.title: À quelle distance les modèles de vision-langage sont-ils de la construction du monde réel ? Un benchmark pour le raisonnement génératif physique
fr.summary: L'étalon DreamHouse teste le raisonnement génératif physique des modèles de vision-langage—synthétiser des artefacts satisfaisant les contraintes structurelles et de conformité du code—plutôt que la plausibilité visuelle, en utilisant 26 000 structures de charpente bois vérifiées. Les expériences révèlent des lacunes de capacités substantielles invisibles dans les métriques du réalisme visuel.

de.title: Wie weit sind Vision-Sprachmodelle von der Konstruktion der realen Welt entfernt? Ein Benchmark für physikalisches generatives Denken
de.summary: Der DreamHouse-Benchmark testet das physikalische generative Denken von Vision-Sprachmodellen – die Synthese von Artefakten, die strukturelle und Code-Compliance-Einschränkungen erfüllen – statt der visuellen Plausibilität, mit 26.000 verifizierten Holzrahmenkonstruktionen. Experimente zeigen erhebliche Leistungslücken, die in visuellen Realismus-Metriken unsichtbar sind.

es.title: ¿A qué distancia están los modelos de lenguaje visual de construir el mundo real? Un benchmark para razonamiento generativo físico
es.summary: El benchmark DreamHouse prueba el razonamiento generativo físico de los modelos de lenguaje visual—sintetizar artefactos que satisfagan restricciones estructurales y de conformidad del código—en lugar de plausibilidad visual, utilizando 26,000 estructuras de marco de madera verificadas. Los experimentos revelan brechas de capacidad sustanciales invisibles en métricas de realismo visual.

title: On the Foundations of Trustworthy Artificial Intelligence
summary: A pure integer-based inference engine achieves platform-deterministic AI computation, proving bitwise identical outputs across ARM and x86 architectures and addressing fundamental violations of determinism in floating-point arithmetic. Distributed verification across geographically separated nodes demonstrates feasibility of deterministic inference at scale.
content:
arXiv:2603.24904v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We prove that platform-deterministic inference is necessary and sufficient for
  trustworthy AI. We formalize this as the Determinism Thesis and introduce trust
  entropy to quantify the cost of non-determinism, proving that verification failure
  probability equals 1 - 2^{-H_T} exactly. We prove a Determinism-Verification
  Collapse: verification under determinism requires O(1) hash comparison; without it,
  the verifier faces an intractable membership problem. IEEE 754 floating-point
  arithmetic fundamentally violates the determinism requirement. We resolve this by
  constructing a pure integer inference engine that achieves bitwise identical output
  across ARM and x86. In 82 cross-architecture tests on models up to 6.7B parameters,
  we observe zero hash mismatches. Four geographically distributed nodes produce
  identical outputs, verified by 356 on-chain attestation transactions. Every major
  trust property of AI systems (fairness, robustness, privacy, safety, alignment)
  presupposes platform determinism. Our system, 99,000 lines of Rust deployed across
  three continents, establishes that AI trust is a question of arithmetic.

zh.title: 论可信人工智能的基础
zh.summary: 纯整数推理引擎实现平台确定性AI计算，证明在ARM和x86架构间的按位相同输出，并解决浮点算术中的根本确定性违规。跨地理分散节点的分布式验证展示了大规模确定性推理的可行性。

fr.title: Sur les fondations de l'intelligence artificielle de confiance
fr.summary: Un moteur d'inférence basé sur des entiers purs réalise un calcul d'IA déterministe au niveau de la plate-forme, prouvant des sorties identiques au bit près sur les architectures ARM et x86 et résolvant les violations fondamentales du déterminisme dans l'arithmétique en virgule flottante. La vérification distribuée entre nœuds géographiquement séparés démontre la faisabilité de l'inférence déterministe à grande échelle.

de.title: Über die Grundlagen vertrauenswürdiger künstlicher Intelligenz
de.summary: Ein reines Integer-basiertes Inferenzmodul erreicht plattformdeterministische KI-Berechnung und beweist bitweise identische Ausgaben über ARM- und x86-Architekturen hinweg, während es grundlegende Verstöße gegen Determinismus in der Gleitkommaarithmetik behebt. Verteilte Verifizierung über geografisch verteilte Knoten demonstriert die Machbarkeit deterministischer Inferenz im großen Maßstab.

es.title: Sobre los fundamentos de la inteligencia artificial confiable
es.summary: Un motor de inferencia basado en enteros puros logra computación de IA determinista a nivel de plataforma, probando salidas idénticas en bit en arquitecturas ARM y x86 y abordando violaciones fundamentales del determinismo en aritmética de punto flotante. La verificación distribuida entre nodos geográficamente separados demuestra la viabilidad de inferencia determinista a escala.

title: LogitScope: A Framework for Analyzing LLM Uncertainty Through Information Metrics
summary: LogitScope quantifies token-level uncertainty in LLM outputs through information metrics like entropy and varentropy, enabling hallucination detection and behavior analysis without labeled data. The lightweight, model-agnostic framework integrates with any HuggingFace model and scales efficiently through lazy evaluation.
content:
arXiv:2603.24929v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Understanding and quantifying uncertainty in large language model (LLM) outputs is critical for reliable deployment. However, traditional evaluation approaches provide limited insight into model confidence at individual token positions during generation. To address this issue, we introduce LogitScope, a lightweight framework for analyzing LLM uncertainty through token-level information metrics computed from probability distributions. By measuring metrics such as entropy and varentropy at each generation step, LogitScope reveals patterns in model confidence, identifies potential hallucinations, and exposes decision points where models exhibit high uncertainty, all without requiring labeled data or semantic interpretation. We demonstrate LogitScope's utility across diverse applications including uncertainty quantification, model behavior analysis, and production monitoring. The framework is model-agnostic, computationally efficient through lazy evaluation, and compatible with any HuggingFace model, enabling both researchers and practitioners to inspect LLM behavior during inference.

zh.title: LogitScope：通过信息指标分析大语言模型不确定性的框架
zh.summary: LogitScope通过熵和方差熵等信息指标量化大语言模型输出中的令牌级不确定性，无需标记数据即可实现幻觉检测和行为分析。这个轻量级、模型无关的框架与任何HuggingFace模型集成，并通过惰性评估高效扩展。

fr.title: LogitScope : Un cadre pour analyser l'incertitude des LLM grâce aux métriques d'information
fr.summary: LogitScope quantifie l'incertitude au niveau du token dans les sorties des LLM par le biais de métriques d'information comme l'entropie et la varentropie, permettant la détection d'hallucinations et l'analyse comportementale sans données étiquetées. Le cadre léger et indépendant du modèle s'intègre à n'importe quel modèle HuggingFace et évolue efficacement grâce à l'évaluation paresseuse.

de.title: LogitScope: Ein Framework zur Analyse der LLM-Unsicherheit durch Informationsmetriken
de.summary: LogitScope quantifiziert Token-Ebenen-Unsicherheit in LLM-Ausgaben durch Informationsmetriken wie Entropie und Varentropie, ermöglicht Halluzinations-Erkennung und Verhaltensanalyse ohne gekennzeichnete Daten. Das leichte, modellunabhängige Framework integriert sich mit jedem HuggingFace-Modell und skaliert effizient durch verzögerte Evaluierung.

es.title: LogitScope: Un marco para analizar la incertidumbre de LLM a través de métricas de información
es.summary: LogitScope cuantifica la incertidumbre a nivel de token en salidas de LLM mediante métricas de información como entropía y varentropía, permitiendo detección de alucinaciones y análisis de comportamiento sin datos etiquetados. El framework ligero e independiente del modelo se integra con cualquier modelo de HuggingFace y se escala eficientemente mediante evaluación perezosa.

title: Decoding Market Emotions in Cryptocurrency Tweets via Predictive Statement Classification with Machine Learning and Transformers
summary: A two-stage classification framework identifies predictive statements in cryptocurrency tweets and categorizes them as bullish, bearish, or neutral, combining ML models with emotion analysis. GPT-based balancing and data augmentation significantly enhance performance, revealing distinct emotional patterns predicting price movements.
content:
arXiv:2603.24933v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The growing prominence of cryptocurrencies has triggered widespread public engagement and increased speculative activity, particularly on social media platforms. This study introduces a novel classification framework for identifying predictive statements in cryptocurrency-related tweets, focusing on five popular cryptocurrencies: Cardano, Matic, Binance, Ripple, and Fantom. The classification process is divided into two stages: Task 1 involves binary classification to distinguish between Predictive and Non-Predictive statements. Tweets identified as Predictive proceed to Task 2, where they are further categorized as Incremental, Decremental, or Neutral. To build a robust dataset, we combined manual and GPT-based annotation methods and utilized SenticNet to extract emotion features corresponding to each prediction category. To address class imbalance, GPT-generated paraphrasing was employed for data augmentation. We evaluated a wide range of machine learning, deep learning, and transformer-based models across both tasks. The results show that GPT-based balancing significantly enhanced model performance, with transformer models achieving the highest F1-score in Task 1, while traditional machine learning models performed best in Task 2. Furthermore, our emotion analysis revealed distinct emotional patterns associated with each prediction category across the different cryptocurrencies.

zh.title: 通过机器学习和Transformers的预测声明分类解读加密货币推文中的市场情绪
zh.summary: 一个两阶段分类框架识别加密货币推文中的预测性陈述，并将其分为看涨、看跌或中立，结合机器学习模型与情感分析。基于GPT的平衡和数据增强显著提高性能，揭示预测价格波动的独特情感模式。

fr.title: Décoder les Émotions du Marché dans les Tweets de Cryptomonnaies via la Classification des Déclarations Prédictives avec l'Apprentissage Automatique et les Transformers
fr.summary: Un cadre de classification en deux étapes identifie les déclarations prédictives dans les tweets de cryptomonnaies et les catégorise comme haussières, baissières ou neutres, combinant les modèles ML avec l'analyse des émotions. L'équilibrage basé sur GPT et l'augmentation des données améliorent considérablement les performances, révélant des modèles émotionnels distincts prédisant les mouvements de prix.

de.title: Dekodierung von Marktgefühlen in Kryptowährungs-Tweets durch Klassifizierung prädikativer Aussagen mit maschinellem Lernen und Transformern
de.summary: Ein zweistufiges Klassifizierungsgerüst identifiziert prädiktive Aussagen in Kryptowährungs-Tweets und kategorisiert sie als optimistisch, pessimistisch oder neutral, wobei ML-Modelle mit Emotionsanalyse kombiniert werden. GPT-basierte Ausgleichung und Datenerweiterung verbessern die Leistung erheblich und offenbaren unterschiedliche emotionale Muster, die Preisbewegungen vorhersagen.

es.title: Descifrando Emociones de Mercado en Tweets de Criptomonedas mediante Clasificación de Declaraciones Predictivas con Aprendizaje Automático y Transformers
es.summary: Un marco de clasificación de dos etapas identifica declaraciones predictivas en tweets de criptomonedas y las categoriza como alcistas, bajistas o neutrales, combinando modelos de ML con análisis de emociones. El equilibrado basado en GPT y la aumentación de datos mejoran significativamente el rendimiento, revelando patrones emocionales distintos que predicen movimientos de precios.

title: FinMCP-Bench: Benchmarking LLM Agents for Real-World Financial Tool Use under the Model Context Protocol
summary: FinMCP-Bench provides a standardized benchmark with 613 samples across 10 financial scenarios for evaluating LLM agents on real-world tool use with financial Model Context Protocols. The benchmark includes single-tool, multi-tool, and multi-turn tasks to assess tool invocation accuracy and reasoning capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.24943v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This paper introduces \textbf{FinMCP-Bench}, a novel benchmark for evaluating large language models (LLMs) in solving real-world financial problems through tool invocation of financial model context protocols. FinMCP-Bench contains 613 samples spanning 10 main scenarios and 33 sub-scenarios, featuring both real and synthetic user queries to ensure diversity and authenticity. It incorporates 65 real financial MCPs and three types of samples, single tool, multi-tool, and multi-turn, allowing evaluation of models across different levels of task complexity. Using this benchmark, we systematically assess a range of mainstream LLMs and propose metrics that explicitly measure tool invocation accuracy and reasoning capabilities. FinMCP-Bench provides a standardized, practical, and challenging testbed for advancing research on financial LLM agents.

zh.title: FinMCP-Bench：基于模型上下文协议的真实金融工具使用LLM代理基准测试
zh.summary: FinMCP-Bench提供了一个标准化的基准测试，包含613个样本，涵盖10个金融场景，用于评估LLM代理在使用金融模型上下文协议进行真实工具使用的能力。该基准包括单工具、多工具和多轮任务，以评估工具调用准确性和推理能力。

fr.title: FinMCP-Bench : Évaluation des Agents LLM pour l'Utilisation Réelle d'Outils Financiers selon le Protocole de Contexte de Modèle
fr.summary: FinMCP-Bench fournit un benchmark standardisé avec 613 exemples à travers 10 scénarios financiers pour évaluer les agents LLM sur l'utilisation réelle d'outils avec les protocoles de contexte de modèle financier. Le benchmark comprend des tâches mono-outil, multi-outils et multi-tours pour évaluer la précision de l'invocation d'outils et les capacités de raisonnement.

de.title: FinMCP-Bench: Benchmarking von LLM-Agenten für die reale Verwendung von Finanzwerkzeugen unter dem Model Context Protocol
de.summary: FinMCP-Bench bietet einen standardisierten Benchmark mit 613 Stichproben über 10 Finanzszenarien zur Bewertung von LLM-Agenten bei der realen Nutzung von Werkzeugen mit Finanz-Model-Context-Protokollen. Der Benchmark umfasst Einzelwerkzeug-, Mehrwerkzeug- und mehrstufige Aufgaben zur Bewertung der Werkzeugaufrufsgenauigkeit und Argumentationsfähigkeiten.

es.title: FinMCP-Bench: Evaluación de Agentes LLM para Uso Real de Herramientas Financieras bajo el Protocolo de Contexto de Modelo
es.summary: FinMCP-Bench proporciona un benchmark estandarizado con 613 muestras en 10 escenarios financieros para evaluar agentes LLM en el uso real de herramientas con Protocolos de Contexto de Modelo Financiero. El benchmark incluye tareas de una herramienta, múltiples herramientas y múltiples turnos para evaluar la precisión de invocación de herramientas y las capacidades de razonamiento.

title: Shopping with a Platform AI Assistant: Who Adopts, When in the Journey, and What For
summary: Analysis of 31 million Ctrip users shows platform AI assistant adoption peaks among older and female users—reversing typical AI demographics—with AI chat complementing rather than replacing search. Users employ the assistant primarily for exploratory tasks like attraction discovery, with interleaved chat-search patterns most common.
content:
arXiv:2603.24947v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This paper provides some of the first large-scale descriptive evidence on how consumers adopt and use platform-embedded shopping AI in e-commerce. Using data on 31 million users of Ctrip, China's largest online travel platform, we study "Wendao," an LLM-based AI assistant integrated into the platform. We document three empirical regularities. First, adoption is highest among older consumers, female users, and highly engaged existing users, reversing the younger, male-dominated profile commonly documented for general-purpose AI tools. Second, AI chat appears in the same broad phase of the purchase journey as traditional search and well before order placement; among journeys containing both chat and search, the most common pattern is interleaving, with users moving back and forth between the two modalities. Third, consumers disproportionately use the assistant for exploratory, hard-to-keyword tasks: attraction queries account for 42% of observed chat requests, and chat intent varies systematically with both the timing of chat relative to search and the category of products later purchased within the same journey. These findings suggest that embedded shopping AI functions less as a substitute for conventional search than as a complementary interface for exploratory product discovery in e-commerce.

zh.title: 使用平台AI助手购物：谁采用、在旅程中何时采用以及用途是什么
zh.summary: 对3100万携程用户的分析表明，平台AI助手的采用在年长和女性用户中达到峰值，这与典型的AI人口统计相反，AI聊天是对搜索的补充而非替代。用户主要将助手用于探索性任务，如景点发现，交错的聊天-搜索模式最为常见。

fr.title: Faire ses Achats avec un Assistant IA de Plateforme : Qui Adopte, Quand dans le Parcours, et Pour Quoi Faire
fr.summary: L'analyse de 31 millions d'utilisateurs de Ctrip montre que l'adoption de l'assistant IA de plateforme culmine parmi les utilisateurs plus âgés et les femmes—inversant les données démographiques typiques de l'IA—avec le chat IA complétant plutôt que remplaçant la recherche. Les utilisateurs emploient principalement l'assistant pour des tâches exploratoires comme la découverte d'attractions, avec des modèles de chat-recherche entrelacés les plus courants.

de.title: Einkaufen mit einem Plattform-KI-Assistenten: Wer Adoptiert, Wann in der Reise und Wofür
de.summary: Die Analyse von 31 Millionen Ctrip-Benutzern zeigt, dass die Einführung des Plattform-KI-Assistenten bei älteren und weiblichen Benutzern ihren Höhepunkt erreicht—was die typische KI-Demografie umkehrt—wobei KI-Chat die Suche ergänzt statt sie zu ersetzen. Benutzer setzen den Assistenten hauptsächlich für explorative Aufgaben wie Sehenswürdigkeitserkennung ein, wobei verflochtene Chat-Such-Muster am häufigsten sind.

es.title: Comprar con un Asistente IA de Plataforma: Quién Adopta, Cuándo en el Viaje y Para Qué
es.summary: El análisis de 31 millones de usuarios de Ctrip muestra que la adopción del asistente IA de plataforma alcanza su pico entre usuarios mayores y mujeres, invirtiéndose los datos demográficos típicos de la IA, con el chat de IA complementando en lugar de reemplazar la búsqueda. Los usuarios emplean el asistente principalmente para tareas exploratorias como descubrimiento de atracciones, con patrones de chat-búsqueda intercalados más comunes.

title: Can MLLMs Read Students' Minds? Unpacking Multimodal Error Analysis in Handwritten Math
summary: Researchers introduced ScratchMath, a benchmark for evaluating multimodal large language models' ability to diagnose errors in handwritten mathematics from Chinese students. The study found significant performance gaps between MLLMs and human experts, with proprietary models outperforming open-source alternatives, revealing limitations in current AI systems for educational assessment.
content:
arXiv:2603.24961v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Assessing student handwritten scratchwork is crucial for personalized educational feedback but presents unique challenges due to diverse handwriting, complex layouts, and varied problem-solving approaches. Existing educational NLP primarily focuses on textual responses and neglects the complexity and multimodality inherent in authentic handwritten scratchwork. Current multimodal large language models (MLLMs) excel at visual reasoning but typically adopt an "examinee perspective", prioritizing generating correct answers rather than diagnosing student errors. To bridge these gaps, we introduce ScratchMath, a novel benchmark specifically designed for explaining and classifying errors in authentic handwritten mathematics scratchwork. Our dataset comprises 1,720 mathematics samples from Chinese primary and middle school students, supporting two key tasks: Error Cause Explanation (ECE) and Error Cause Classification (ECC), with seven defined error types. The dataset is meticulously annotated through rigorous human-machine collaborative approaches involving multiple stages of expert labeling, review, and verification. We systematically evaluate 16 leading MLLMs on ScratchMath, revealing significant performance gaps relative to human experts, especially in visual recognition and logical reasoning. Proprietary models notably outperform open-source models, with large reasoning models showing strong potential for error explanation. All evaluation data and frameworks are publicly available to facilitate further research.

zh.title: 多模态大型语言模型能读懂学生的想法吗？揭开手写数学错误分析的神秘面纱
zh.summary: 研究人员引入了ScratchMath，这是一个评估多模态大型语言模型能否诊断中国学生手写数学错误的基准。该研究发现多模态大型语言模型与人类专家之间存在显著性能差距，专有模型的表现优于开源替代品，揭示了当前人工智能系统在教育评估中的局限性。

fr.title: Les MLLM peuvent-ils lire les pensées des étudiants ? Démystifier l'analyse des erreurs multimodales en mathématiques manuscrites
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont présenté ScratchMath, une référence pour évaluer la capacité des modèles de langage multimodaux volumineux à diagnostiquer les erreurs en mathématiques manuscrites chez les étudiants chinois. L'étude a révélé des écarts de performance significatifs entre les MLLM et les experts humains, les modèles propriétaires surpassant les alternatives open-source, révélant les limitations des systèmes d'IA actuels pour l'évaluation éducative.

de.title: Können MLLMs die Gedanken von Schülern lesen? Entmystifizierung der multimodalen Fehleranalyse in der handgeschriebenen Mathematik
de.summary: Forscher führten ScratchMath ein, einen Maßstab zur Bewertung der Fähigkeit großer multimodaler Sprachmodelle, Fehler in der handgeschriebenen Mathematik chinesischer Schüler zu diagnostizieren. Die Studie zeigte signifikante Leistungsunterschiede zwischen MLLMs und menschlichen Experten, wobei proprietäre Modelle Open-Source-Alternativen übertroffen, was die Grenzen aktueller KI-Systeme für die Bildungsbewertung offenbarte.

es.title: ¿Pueden los MLLM leer la mente de los estudiantes? Desmitificando el análisis multimodal de errores en matemáticas manuscritas
es.summary: Los investigadores presentaron ScratchMath, una referencia para evaluar la capacidad de los modelos grandes de lenguaje multimodal para diagnosticar errores en matemáticas manuscritas de estudiantes chinos. El estudio encontró brechas de rendimiento significativas entre los MLLM y los expertos humanos, con modelos propietarios superando a las alternativas de código abierto, revelando limitaciones en los sistemas de IA actuales para la evaluación educativa.

title: Design Once, Deploy at Scale: Template-Driven ML Development for Large Model Ecosystems
summary: Meta researchers presented the Standard Model Template (SMT), a framework that reduces ML innovation deployment complexity across large model ecosystems from O(n·2^k) to O(n+k). Testing in Meta's production ads system showed 0.63% performance improvement, 92% reduction in iteration time, and 6.3× faster technique adoption.
content:
arXiv:2603.24963v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Modern computational advertising platforms typically rely on recommendation systems to predict user responses, such as click-through rates, conversion rates, and other optimization events. To support a wide variety of product surfaces and advertiser goals, these platforms frequently maintain an extensive ecosystem of machine learning (ML) models. However, operating at this scale creates significant development and efficiency challenges. Substantial engineering effort is required to regularly refresh ML models and propagate new techniques, which results in long latencies when deploying ML innovations across the ecosystem.
  We present a large-scale empirical study comparing model performance, efficiency, and ML technique propagation between a standardized model-building approach and independent per-model optimization in recommendation systems. To facilitate this standardization, we propose the Standard Model Template (SMT) -- a framework that generates high-performance models adaptable to diverse data distributions and optimization events. By utilizing standardized, composable ML model components, SMT reduces technique propagation complexity from $O(n \cdot 2^k)$ to $O(n + k)$ where $n$ is the number of models and $k$ the number of techniques.
  Evaluating an extensive suite of models over four global development cycles within Meta's production ads ranking ecosystem, our results demonstrate: (1) a 0.63% average improvement in cross-entropy at neutral serving capacity, (2) a 92% reduction in per-model iteration engineering time, and (3) a $6.3\times$ increase in technique-model pair adoption throughput. These findings challenge the conventional wisdom that diverse optimization goals inherently require diversified ML model design.

zh.title: 一次设计，大规模部署：大型模型生态系统的模板驱动机器学习开发
zh.summary: Meta研究人员推出了标准模型模板（SMT），这是一个将大型模型生态系统中的机器学习创新部署复杂性从O(n·2^k)降低到O(n+k)的框架。在Meta生产广告系统中的测试显示性能提高0.63%，迭代时间减少92%，技术采用速度快6.3倍。

fr.title: Concevoir une fois, déployer à l'échelle : développement du ML basé sur des modèles pour les grands écosystèmes de modèles
fr.summary: Les chercheurs de Meta ont présenté le Standard Model Template (SMT), un cadre qui réduit la complexité du déploiement d'innovations ML dans les grands écosystèmes de modèles de O(n·2^k) à O(n+k). Les tests dans le système d'annonces de production de Meta ont montré une amélioration des performances de 0,63%, une réduction de 92% du temps d'itération et une adoption des techniques 6,3 fois plus rapide.

de.title: Einmal entwerfen, in großem Maßstab bereitstellen: Vorlagengesteuerte ML-Entwicklung für große Modell-Ökosysteme
de.summary: Meta-Forscher präsentierten die Standard Model Template (SMT), ein Framework, das die Komplexität der ML-Innovationsbereitstellung in großen Modell-Ökosystemen von O(n·2^k) auf O(n+k) reduziert. Tests in Metas Produktionsanzeigensystem zeigten eine Leistungsverbesserung von 0,63%, eine Reduzierung der Iterationszeit um 92% und eine 6,3-mal schnellere Technikadoption.

es.title: Diseñar una vez, desplegar a escala: desarrollo de aprendizaje automático impulsado por plantillas para grandes ecosistemas de modelos
es.summary: Los investigadores de Meta presentaron la Plantilla de Modelo Estándar (SMT), un marco que reduce la complejidad del despliegue de innovación de aprendizaje automático en grandes ecosistemas de modelos de O(n·2^k) a O(n+k). Las pruebas en el sistema de anuncios de producción de Meta mostraron una mejora de rendimiento del 0,63%, una reducción del 92% en el tiempo de iteración y una adopción de técnicas 6,3 veces más rápida.

title: The Anatomy of Uncertainty in LLMs
summary: Researchers propose an uncertainty decomposition framework that breaks down LLM uncertainty into three semantic components: input ambiguity, knowledge gaps, and decoding randomness. This framework helps audit LLM reliability, detect hallucinations, and enables targeted improvements for more trustworthy systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.24967v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Understanding why a large language model (LLM) is uncertain about the response is important for their reliable deployment. Current approaches, which either provide a single uncertainty score or rely on the classical aleatoric-epistemic dichotomy, fail to offer actionable insights for improving the generative model. Recent studies have also shown that such methods are not enough for understanding uncertainty in LLMs. In this work, we advocate for an uncertainty decomposition framework that dissects LLM uncertainty into three distinct semantic components: (i) input ambiguity, arising from ambiguous prompts; (ii) knowledge gaps, caused by insufficient parametric evidence; and (iii) decoding randomness, stemming from stochastic sampling. Through a series of experiments we demonstrate that the dominance of these components can shift across model size and task. Our framework provides a better understanding to audit LLM reliability and detect hallucinations, paving the way for targeted interventions and more trustworthy systems.

zh.title: LLM中的不确定性解剖学
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了一个不确定性分解框架，将LLM不确定性分解为三个语义成分：输入歧义、知识缺陷和解码随机性。该框架有助于审计LLM可靠性、检测幻觉，并能够进行有针对性的改进以构建更值得信赖的系统。

fr.title: L'Anatomie de l'Incertitude dans les LLM
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent un cadre de décomposition de l'incertitude qui décompose l'incertitude des LLM en trois composantes sémantiques : l'ambiguïté des entrées, les lacunes de connaissances et l'aléatoire du décodage. Ce cadre aide à auditer la fiabilité des LLM, à détecter les hallucinations et permet des améliorations ciblées pour des systèmes plus fiables.

de.title: Die Anatomie der Unsicherheit in LLMs
de.summary: Forscher schlagen ein Unsicherheitszerlegungsrahmenwerk vor, das die LLM-Unsicherheit in drei semantische Komponenten zerlegt: Eingabeuneindeutigkeit, Wissenslücken und Dekodierungszufälligkeit. Dieses Framework hilft, die Zuverlässigkeit von LLMs zu überprüfen, Halluzinationen zu erkennen und ermöglicht gezielte Verbesserungen für vertrauenswürdigere Systeme.

es.title: La Anatomía de la Incertidumbre en LLMs
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen un marco de descomposición de incertidumbre que desglosa la incertidumbre de LLM en tres componentes semánticos: ambigüedad de entrada, brechas de conocimiento e aleatoriedad de decodificación. Este marco ayuda a auditar la confiabilidad de LLM, detectar alucinaciones y permite mejoras específicas para sistemas más confiables.

title: Rethinking Failure Attribution in Multi-Agent Systems: A Multi-Perspective Benchmark and Evaluation
summary: The paper introduces MP-Bench, a new benchmark for multi-perspective failure attribution in multi-agent systems that accounts for attribution ambiguity from complex inter-agent dependencies. Results show prior conclusions about LLM debugging struggles were driven by benchmark limitations, highlighting the need for more realistic evaluation protocols.
content:
arXiv:2603.25001v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Failure attribution is essential for diagnosing and improving multi-agent systems (MAS), yet existing benchmarks and methods largely assume a single deterministic root cause for each failure. In practice, MAS failures often admit multiple plausible attributions due to complex inter-agent dependencies and ambiguous execution trajectories. We revisit MAS failure attribution from a multi-perspective standpoint and propose multi-perspective failure attribution, a practical paradigm that explicitly accounts for attribution ambiguity. To support this setting, we introduce MP-Bench, the first benchmark designed for multi-perspective failure attribution in MAS, along with a new evaluation protocol tailored to this paradigm. Through extensive experiments, we find that prior conclusions suggesting LLMs struggle with failure attribution are largely driven by limitations in existing benchmark designs. Our results highlight the necessity of multi-perspective benchmarks and evaluation protocols for realistic and reliable MAS debugging.

zh.title: 重新思考多智能体系统中的故障归因：多视角基准和评估
zh.summary: 该论文介绍了MP-Bench，这是一个用于多智能体系统中多视角故障归因的新基准，考虑了来自复杂智能体间依赖的归因模糊性。结果表明关于LLM调试困难的先前结论是由基准限制驱动的，突出了对更现实评估协议的需求。

fr.title: Repenser l'Attribution des Défaillances dans les Systèmes Multi-Agents : Un Benchmark et une Évaluation Multi-Perspectives
fr.summary: L'article introduit MP-Bench, un nouveau benchmark pour l'attribution des défaillances multi-perspectives dans les systèmes multi-agents qui tient compte de l'ambiguïté d'attribution provenant des dépendances inter-agents complexes. Les résultats montrent que les conclusions antérieures sur les difficultés de débogage des LLM étaient motivées par les limitations du benchmark, soulignant le besoin de protocoles d'évaluation plus réalistes.

de.title: Umdenken der Fehlerzuschreibung in Multi-Agent-Systemen: Ein Multi-Perspektive-Benchmark und Bewertung
de.summary: Das Papier stellt MP-Bench vor, einen neuen Benchmark für mehrperspektivische Fehlerzuschreibung in Multi-Agent-Systemen, der die Zuschreibungsuneindeutigkeit aus komplexen Agent-zu-Agent-Abhängigkeiten berücksichtigt. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass frühere Schlussfolgerungen über LLM-Debugging-Schwierigkeiten durch Benchmark-Einschränkungen getrieben wurden, was die Notwendigkeit realistischerer Bewertungsprotokolle hervorhebt.

es.title: Repensar la Atribución de Fallos en Sistemas Multi-Agente: Un Benchmark y Evaluación Multi-Perspectiva
es.summary: El artículo introduce MP-Bench, un nuevo benchmark para la atribución de fallas multi-perspectiva en sistemas multi-agente que considera la ambigüedad de atribución derivada de dependencias inter-agente complejas. Los resultados muestran que las conclusiones previas sobre las dificultades de depuración de LLM fueron impulsadas por limitaciones del benchmark, destacando la necesidad de protocolos de evaluación más realistas.

title: A Public Theory of Distillation Resistance via Constraint-Coupled Reasoning Architectures
summary: This paper presents a public theoretical framework for reducing risks that useful AI capabilities can be transferred more cheaply than their original governance structures through distillation. It proposes constraint-coupled reasoning architectures where high-level capability couples to internal stability constraints, making distillation less valuable as a shortcut.
content:
arXiv:2603.25022v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Knowledge distillation, model extraction, and behavior transfer have become central concerns in frontier AI. The main risk is not merely copying, but the possibility that useful capability can be transferred more cheaply than the governance structure that originally accompanied it. This paper presents a public, trade-secret-safe theoretical framework for reducing that asymmetry at the architectural level. The core claim is that distillation becomes less valuable as a shortcut when high-level capability is coupled to internal stability constraints that shape state transitions over time. To formalize this idea, the paper introduces a constraint-coupled reasoning framework with four elements: bounded transition burden, path-load accumulation, dynamically evolving feasible regions, and a capability-stability coupling condition. The paper is intentionally public-safe: it omits proprietary implementation details, training recipes, thresholds, hidden-state instrumentation, deployment procedures, and confidential system design choices. The contribution is therefore theoretical rather than operational. It offers a falsifiable architectural thesis, a clear threat model, and a set of experimentally testable hypotheses for future work on distillation resistance, alignment, and model governance.

zh.title: 通过约束耦合推理架构的蒸馏抵抗公开理论
zh.summary: 本论文提出了一个公开的理论框架，用于降低有用的AI能力通过蒸馏以更低成本转移而不遵循其原始治理结构的风险。它提出了约束耦合推理架构，其中高层能力与内部稳定性约束相耦合，使蒸馏作为捷径变得价值更低。

fr.title: Une Théorie Publique de la Résistance à la Distillation via des Architectures de Raisonnement Couplées par Contrainte
fr.summary: Cet article présente un cadre théorique public pour réduire les risques que les capacités d'IA utiles puissent être transférées à moindre coût que leurs structures de gouvernance originales par la distillation. Il propose des architectures de raisonnement couplées par contrainte où la capacité de haut niveau est couplée à des contraintes de stabilité interne, rendant la distillation moins précieuse comme raccourci.

de.title: Eine öffentliche Theorie der Destillationsresistenz durch Constraint-gekoppelte Reasoning-Architekturen
de.summary: Dieses Papier präsentiert ein öffentliches theoretisches Framework zur Verringerung des Risikos, dass nützliche KI-Fähigkeiten durch Destillation billiger transferiert werden können als ihre ursprünglichen Governance-Strukturen. Es schlägt Constraint-gekoppelte Reasoning-Architekturen vor, bei denen High-Level-Fähigkeit an interne Stabilitätsbeschränkungen gekoppelt ist, wodurch Destillation als Abkürzung weniger wertvoll wird.

es.title: Una Teoría Pública de la Resistencia a la Destilación a través de Arquitecturas de Razonamiento Acopladas por Restricción
es.summary: Este artículo presenta un marco teórico público para reducir los riesgos de que las capacidades útiles de IA puedan transferirse más barato que sus estructuras de gobernanza originales a través de la destilación. Propone arquitecturas de razonamiento acopladas por restricción donde la capacidad de alto nivel se acopla a restricciones de estabilidad interna, haciendo que la destilación sea menos valiosa como atajo.

title: System-Anchored Knee Estimation for Low-Cost Context Window Selection in PDE Forecasting
summary: Researchers introduced System-Anchored Knee Estimation (SAKE), a method for efficient context-window selection in autoregressive neural PDE simulators. SAKE achieves 67.8% exact matches and 94.9% search-cost savings compared to other low-cost selection methods across multiple benchmark families.
content:
arXiv:2603.25025v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Autoregressive neural PDE simulators predict the evolution of physical fields one step at a time from a finite history, but low-cost context-window selection for such simulators remains an unformalized problem. Existing approaches to context-window selection in time-series forecasting include exhaustive validation, direct low-cost search, and system-theoretic memory estimation, but they are either expensive, brittle, or not directly aligned with downstream rollout performance. We formalize explicit context-window selection for fixed-window autoregressive neural PDE simulators as an independent low-cost algorithmic problem, and propose \textbf{System-Anchored Knee Estimation (SAKE)}, a two-stage method that first identifies a small structured candidate set from physically interpretable system anchors and then performs knee-aware downstream selection within it. Across all eight PDEBench families evaluated under the shared \(L\in\{1,\dots,16\}\) protocol, SAKE is the strongest overall matched-budget low-cost selector among the evaluated methods, achieving 67.8\% Exact, 91.7\% Within-1, 6.1\% mean regret@knee, and a cost ratio of 0.051 (94.9\% normalized search-cost savings).

zh.title: 系统锚定膝点估计用于偏微分方程预报的低成本上下文窗口选择
zh.summary: 研究人员引入了系统锚定膝点估计(SAKE),这是一种在自回归神经PDE模拟器中进行高效上下文窗口选择的方法。与多个基准族中的其他低成本选择方法相比,SAKE达到了67.8%的精确匹配和94.9%的搜索成本节省。

fr.title: Estimation du genou ancrée au système pour la sélection de fenêtre de contexte à faible coût dans la prévision des EDP
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont introduit l'estimation du genou ancrée au système (SAKE), une méthode de sélection efficace de fenêtre de contexte dans les simulateurs PDE neuraux autorégressifs. SAKE atteint 67,8% de correspondances exactes et 94,9% d'économies de coûts de recherche par rapport à d'autres méthodes de sélection à faible coût dans plusieurs familles de benchmarks.

de.title: Systemverankerte Knieestimation für kostengünstige Kontextfensterauswahl bei PDE-Prognosen
de.summary: Forscher führten die systemverankerte Knnieestimation (SAKE) ein, eine Methode zur effizienten Kontextfensterauswahl in autoregressiven neuronalen PDE-Simulatoren. SAKE erreicht 67,8% genaue Übereinstimmungen und 94,9% Suchkosteneinsparungen im Vergleich zu anderen kostengünstigen Auswahlmethoden über mehrere Benchmark-Familien hinweg.

es.title: Estimación de rodilla ancorada al sistema para la selección de ventana de contexto de bajo costo en pronóstico de PDE
es.summary: Los investigadores introdujeron la estimación de rodilla ancorada al sistema (SAKE), un método para la selección eficiente de ventana de contexto en simuladores PDE neurales autoregresivos. SAKE logra 67,8% de coincidencias exactas y 94,9% de ahorros en costos de búsqueda en comparación con otros métodos de selección de bajo costo en múltiples familias de puntos de referencia.

title: From Stateless to Situated: Building a Psychological World for LLM-Based Emotional Support
