summary: Researchers propose LEKIA 2.0, a situated LLM architecture that separates cognitive and executive layers to maintain stable user situation representations and consent boundaries during multi-turn emotional support interactions. The system achieved approximately 31% improvement over prompt-only baselines in deep intervention completion.
content:
arXiv:2603.25031v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In psychological support and emotional companionship scenarios, the core limitation of large language models (LLMs) lies not merely in response quality, but in their reliance on local next-token prediction, which prevents them from maintaining the temporal continuity, stage awareness, and user consent boundaries required for multi-turn intervention. This stateless characteristic makes systems prone to premature advancement, stage misalignment, and boundary violations in continuous dialogue. To address this problem, we argue that the key challenge in process-oriented emotional support is not simply generating natural language, but constructing a sustainably updatable external situational structure for the model. We therefore propose LEKIA 2.0, a situated LLM architecture that separates the cognitive layer from the executive layer, thereby decoupling situational modeling from intervention execution. This design enables the system to maintain stable representations of the user's situation and consent boundaries throughout ongoing interaction. To evaluate this process-control capability, we further introduce a Static-to-Dynamic online evaluation protocol for multi-turn interaction. LEKIA achieved an average absolute improvement of approximately 31% over prompt-only baselines in deep intervention loop completion. The results suggest that an external situational structure is a key enabling condition for building stable, controllable, and situated emotional support systems.

zh.title: 从无状态到情境化:为基于LLM的情感支持构建心理世界
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了LEKIA 2.0,一种情境化的LLM架构,它分离认知层和执行层,以在多轮情感支持互动中维持稳定的用户情境表示和同意边界。该系统在深度干预完成方面比纯提示基线实现了约31%的改进。

fr.title: Du stateless au situé: Construire un monde psychologique pour le support émotionnel basé sur LLM
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent LEKIA 2.0, une architecture LLM située qui sépare les couches cognitives et exécutives pour maintenir des représentations de situation utilisateur stables et des limites de consentement lors des interactions de support émotionnel multi-tour. Le système a réalisé une amélioration d'environ 31% par rapport aux baselines seulement basées sur des invites dans l'achèvement d'interventions profondes.

de.title: Von Stateless zu Situated: Aufbau einer psychologischen Welt für LLM-basierte emotionale Unterstützung
de.summary: Forscher schlagen LEKIA 2.0 vor, eine situierte LLM-Architektur, die kognitive und exekutive Schichten trennt, um stabile Benutzer-Situationsdarstellungen und Zustimmungsgrenzen während mehrteiliger emotionaler Unterstützungsinteraktionen zu erhalten. Das System erreichte eine Verbesserung von etwa 31% gegenüber Baselines mit nur Prompt-Eingabe bei der Durchführung tiefer Interventionen.

es.title: De sin estado a situado: Construir un mundo psicológico para soporte emocional basado en LLM
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen LEKIA 2.0, una arquitectura LLM situada que separa capas cognitivas y ejecutivas para mantener representaciones de situación de usuario estables y límites de consentimiento durante interacciones de soporte emocional de múltiples turnos. El sistema logró una mejora de aproximadamente 31% sobre las líneas base solo de indicaciones en la finalización de intervenciones profundas.

title: Mechanistically Interpreting Compression in Vision-Language Models
summary: Using causal circuit analysis and feature comparisons, researchers examined how pruning and quantization affect vision-language models' internal computations and safety behaviors. The study found that pruning causes sharp drops in refusal behavior, revealing critical safety implications of model compression choices.
content:
arXiv:2603.25035v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Compressed vision-language models (VLMs) are widely used to reduce memory and compute costs, making them a suitable choice for real-world deployment. However, compressing these models raises concerns about whether internal computations and safety behaviors are preserved. In this work, we use causal circuit analysis and crosscoder-based feature comparisons to examine how pruning and quantization fundamentally change the internals across representative VLMs. We observe that pruning generally keeps circuit structure intact but rotates and attenuates internal features, while quantization modifies the circuits at a higher level yet leaves the surviving features better aligned. Leveraging this insight, we also introduce VLMSafe-420, a novel benchmark that pairs harmful inputs with matched benign counterfactuals across various safety categories. Our findings show that pruning causes a sharp drop in genuine refusal behavior, suggesting that the choice of compression has safety implications.

zh.title: 视觉语言模型中压缩的机制解释
zh.summary: 使用因果电路分析和特征比较,研究人员研究了修剪和量化如何影响视觉语言模型的内部计算和安全行为。该研究发现修剪导致拒绝行为的急剧下降,揭示了模型压缩选择的关键安全影响。

fr.title: Interprétation mécanistique de la compression dans les modèles de vision-langage
fr.summary: En utilisant l'analyse de circuits causaux et les comparaisons de caractéristiques, les chercheurs ont examiné comment l'élagage et la quantification affectent les calculs internes et les comportements de sécurité des modèles de vision-langage. L'étude a révélé que l'élagage provoque des baisses nettes du comportement de refus, révélant des implications de sécurité critiques des choix de compression de modèles.

de.title: Mechanistische Interpretation der Komprimierung in Vision-Language-Modellen
de.summary: Unter Verwendung von kausaler Schaltungsanalyse und Merkmalvergleichen untersuchten Forscher, wie Beschneidung und Quantisierung die internen Berechnungen und Sicherheitsverhalten von Vision-Language-Modellen beeinflussen. Die Studie zeigte, dass Beschneidung zu starken Rückgängen bei Ablehnungsverhalten führt und kritische Sicherheitsauswirkungen von Modellkomprimierungsentscheidungen offenbart.

es.title: Interpretación mecanicista de la compresión en modelos de visión y lenguaje
es.summary: Utilizando análisis de circuitos causales y comparaciones de características, los investigadores examinaron cómo la poda y la cuantificación afectan los cálculos internos y los comportamientos de seguridad de los modelos de visión y lenguaje. El estudio encontró que la poda causa caídas bruscas en el comportamiento de rechazo, revelando implicaciones críticas de seguridad de las opciones de compresión de modelos.

title: MP-MoE: Matrix Profile-Guided Mixture of Experts for Precipitation Forecasting
summary: Researchers developed MP-MoE, a framework combining intensity loss with Matrix Profile objectives to improve precipitation forecasting in tropical regions where complex topography limits accuracy. Results show MP-MoE outperforms baselines in capturing peak rainfall intensities and preserving storm morphology.
content:
arXiv:2603.25046v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Precipitation forecasting remains a persistent challenge in tropical regions like Vietnam, where complex topography and convective instability often limit the accuracy of Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP) models. While data-driven post-processing is widely used to mitigate these biases, most existing frameworks rely on point-wise objective functions, which suffer from the ``double penalty'' effect under minor temporal misalignments. In this work, we propose the Matrix Profile-guided Mixture of Experts (MP-MoE), a framework that integrates conventional intensity loss with a structural-aware Matrix Profile objective. By leveraging subsequence-level similarity rather than point-wise errors, the proposed loss facilitates more reliable expert selection and mitigates excessive penalization caused by phase shifts. We evaluate MP-MoE on rainfall datasets from two major river basins in Vietnam across multiple horizons, including 1-hour intensity and accumulated rainfall over 12, 24, and 48 hours. Experimental results demonstrate that MP-MoE outperforms raw NWP and baseline learning methods in terms of Mean Critical Success Index (CSI-M) for heavy rainfall events, while significantly reducing Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) values. These findings highlight the framework's efficacy in capturing peak rainfall intensities and preserving the morphological integrity of storm events.

zh.title: MP-MoE：基于矩阵轮廓的混合专家降水预报框架
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了MP-MoE框架，结合强度损失与矩阵轮廓目标来改善复杂地形限制精度的热带地区降水预报。结果表明MP-MoE在捕捉峰值降水强度和保留风暴形态方面优于基准方法。

fr.title: MP-MoE: Mélange d'Experts Guidé par Profil Matriciel pour la Prévision des Précipitations
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont développé MP-MoE, un cadre combinant la perte d'intensité avec les objectifs du profil matriciel pour améliorer la prévision des précipitations dans les régions tropicales où la topographie complexe limite la précision. Les résultats montrent que MP-MoE surpasse les solutions de base pour capturer les intensités maximales de précipitations et préserver la morphologie des tempêtes.

de.title: MP-MoE: Matrixprofil-gesteuerte Mixture of Experts für Niederschlagsvorhersage
de.summary: Forscher entwickelten MP-MoE, ein Framework, das Intensitätsverlust mit Matrixprofilzielen kombiniert, um die Niederschlagsvorhersage in tropischen Regionen zu verbessern, wo komplexe Topografie die Genauigkeit begrenzt. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass MP-MoE Basismethoden beim Erfassen von Spitzenniederschlagsintensitäten und bei der Erhaltung der Sturmmorphologie übertrifft.

es.title: MP-MoE: Mezcla de Expertos Guiada por Perfil Matricial para Pronóstico de Precipitación
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollaron MP-MoE, un marco que combina pérdida de intensidad con objetivos de perfil matricial para mejorar el pronóstico de precipitación en regiones tropicales donde la topografía compleja limita la precisión. Los resultados muestran que MP-MoE supera a los métodos base en la captura de intensidades máximas de precipitación y en la preservación de la morfología de tormentas.

title: Sparse Visual Thought Circuits in Vision-Language Models
summary: Researchers tested whether sparse autoencoder features form modular, composable units in vision-language models and found they often don't, as intervening on combined feature sets causes unintended output drift and degradation. This challenges assumptions underlying steering methods and clarifies limitations of SAE feature composability.
content:
arXiv:2603.25075v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) improve interpretability in multimodal models, but it remains unclear whether SAE features form modular, composable units for reasoning-an assumption underlying many intervention-based steering methods. We test this modularity hypothesis and find it often fails: intervening on a task-selective feature set can modestly improve reasoning accuracy, while intervening on the union of two such sets reliably induces output drift (large unintended changes in predictions) and degrades accuracy, even under norm-matched perturbations. This non modular circuit interference is consistent with shared internal pathways where feature unions amplify activation shifts. We develop a reproducible causal pipeline to localize and test these sparse visual thought circuits in Qwen3-VL-8B. On a controlled synthetic benchmark with seven task types and three difficulty levels, linear probes identify a mid decoder locus for task type information. We train SAEs at this layer, construct task-selective sets via an explicit rule, and perform inference time scaling and ablation while quantifying accuracy and drift. Our findings-validated with bootstrapped subsamples and permutation controls, and replicated across multiple VLM families and five diverse datasets clarify the boundaries of SAE feature composability and provide a rigorous diagnostic framework for more reliable VLM control.

zh.title: 视觉语言模型中的稀疏视觉思维电路
zh.summary: 研究人员测试了稀疏自编码器特征在视觉语言模型中是否形成模块化、可组合的单元，发现通常不会，因为对组合特征集的干预会导致意外的输出漂移和退化。这挑战了转向方法的基础假设，并阐明了自编码器特征可组合性的局限性。

fr.title: Circuits de Pensée Visuelle Clairsemée dans les Modèles Vision-Langage
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont testé si les caractéristiques des autoencodeurs clairsemés forment des unités modulaires et composables dans les modèles vision-langage et ont constaté que ce n'est souvent pas le cas, car l'intervention sur des ensembles de caractéristiques combinées provoque une dérive et une dégradation des résultats non intentionnelles. Cela remet en question les hypothèses sous-jacentes aux méthodes de direction et clarifie les limitations de la composabilité des caractéristiques SAE.

de.title: Sparsame visuelle Gedankenschaltungen in Vision-Language-Modellen
de.summary: Forscher testeten, ob dünne Autoencoder-Merkmale modulare, kombinierbare Einheiten in Vision-Language-Modellen bilden, und stellten fest, dass dies oft nicht der Fall ist, da die Intervention auf kombinierten Merkmalssätzen zu unbeabsichtigtem Output-Drift und Degradation führt. Dies stellt Annahmen zugrunde liegender Steuerungsmethoden in Frage und verdeutlicht Einschränkungen der SAE-Merkmalskombinierbarkeit.

es.title: Circuitos de Pensamiento Visual Disperso en Modelos Visión-Lenguaje
es.summary: Los investigadores probaron si las características del autoencoder disperso forman unidades modulares y componibles en modelos visión-lenguaje y descubrieron que a menudo no lo hacen, ya que la intervención en conjuntos de características combinadas causa una deriva de salida no intencionada y degradación. Esto desafía las suposiciones subyacentes en los métodos de dirección y aclara las limitaciones de la componibilidad de características SAE.

title: ElephantBroker: A Knowledge-Grounded Cognitive Runtime for Trustworthy AI Agents
summary: ElephantBroker is an open-source cognitive runtime that unifies a Neo4j knowledge graph with a Qdrant vector store to provide verifiable, trustworthy agent memory with knowledge provenance tracking. The system implements a comprehensive cognitive loop with safety enforcement and auditability features for high-stakes agent deployments.
content:
arXiv:2603.25097v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Model based agents increasingly operate in high stakes, multi turn settings where factual grounding is critical, yet their memory systems typically rely on flat key value stores or plain vector retrieval with no mechanism to track the provenance or trustworthiness of stored knowledge. We present ElephantBroker, an open source cognitive runtime that unifies a Neo4j knowledge graph with a Qdrant vector store through the Cognee SDK to provide durable, verifiable agent memory. The system implements a complete cognitive loop (store, retrieve, score, compose, protect, learn) comprising a hybrid five source retrieval pipeline, an eleven dimension competitive scoring engine for budget constrained context assembly, a four state evidence verification model, a five stage context lifecycle with goal aware assembly and continuous compaction, a six layer cheap first guard pipeline for safety enforcement, an AI firewall providing enforceable tool call interception and multi tier safety scanning, a nine stage consolidation engine that strengthens useful patterns while decaying noise, and a numeric authority model governing multi organization identity with hierarchical access control. Architectural validation through a comprehensive test suite of over 2,200 tests spanning unit, integration, and end to end levels confirms subsystem correctness. The modular design supports three deployment tiers, five profile presets with inheritance, multi gateway isolation, and a management dashboard for human oversight, enabling configurations from lightweight memory only agents to full cognitive runtimes with enterprise grade safety and auditability.

zh.title: ElephantBroker：面向可信AI代理的知识驱动认知运行时
zh.summary: ElephantBroker是一个开源认知运行时，统一了Neo4j知识图谱和Qdrant向量存储，提供可验证、可信的代理记忆和知识来源追踪。该系统实现了全面的认知循环，具有安全执行和审计功能，适用于高风险代理部署。

fr.title: ElephantBroker: Un Environnement d'Exécution Cognitif Basé sur les Connaissances pour des Agents IA de Confiance
fr.summary: ElephantBroker est un environnement d'exécution cognitif open-source qui unifie un graphe de connaissances Neo4j avec un magasin vectoriel Qdrant pour fournir une mémoire d'agent vérifiable et fiable avec suivi de la provenance des connaissances. Le système implémente une boucle cognitive complète avec application de la sécurité et des fonctionnalités d'auditabilité pour les déploiements d'agents à enjeux élevés.

de.title: ElephantBroker: Eine wissensgestützte kognitive Laufzeitumgebung für vertrauenswürdige KI-Agenten
de.summary: ElephantBroker ist eine Open-Source-Kognitive Laufzeitumgebung, die einen Neo4j-Wissensgraphen mit einem Qdrant-Vektorspeicher vereint, um verifizierbare, vertrauenswürdige Agent-Memory mit Wissensherkunftsrückverfolgung bereitzustellen. Das System implementiert eine umfassende kognitive Schleife mit Sicherheitsdurchsetzung und Audit-Funktionen für hochriskante Agent-Bereitstellungen.

es.title: ElephantBroker: Un Tiempo de Ejecución Cognitivo Basado en Conocimiento para Agentes de IA de Confianza
es.summary: ElephantBroker es un tiempo de ejecución cognitivo de código abierto que unifica un gráfico de conocimiento Neo4j con un almacén vectorial Qdrant para proporcionar memoria de agente verificable y confiable con seguimiento de procedencia del conocimiento. El sistema implementa un bucle cognitivo completo con cumplimiento de seguridad y características de auditoría para despliegues de agentes de alto riesgo.

title: When Sensing Varies with Contexts: Context-as-Transform for Tactile Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning
summary: Researchers propose Context-as-Transform FSCIL (CaT-FSCIL) to address few-shot class-incremental learning in tactile sensing where varying acquisition contexts degrade performance. The method decomposes context into structured and residual components, achieving superior results on tactile sensing benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.25115v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning (FSCIL) can be particularly susceptible to acquisition contexts with only a few labeled samples. A typical scenario is tactile sensing, where the acquisition context ({\it e.g.}, diverse devices, contact state, and interaction settings) degrades performance due to a lack of standardization. In this paper, we propose Context-as-Transform FSCIL (CaT-FSCIL) to tackle the above problem. We decompose the acquisition context into a structured low-dimensional component and a high-dimensional residual component. The former can be easily affected by tactile interaction features, which are modeled as an approximately invertible Context-as-Transform family and handled via inverse-transform canonicalization optimized with a pseudo-context consistency loss. The latter mainly arises from platform and device differences, which can be mitigated with an Uncertainty-Conditioned Prototype Calibration (UCPC) that calibrates biased prototypes and decision boundaries based on context uncertainty. Comprehensive experiments on the standard benchmarks HapTex and LMT108 have demonstrated the superiority of the proposed CaT-FSCIL.

zh.title: 当感知随环境变化时：用于触觉少样本类增量学习的语境即变换方法
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了语境即变换FSCIL (CaT-FSCIL)，以解决触觉感知中的少样本类增量学习问题，其中不同的获取环境会降低性能。该方法将环境分解为结构化和残差分量，在触觉感知基准上取得了优异的结果。

fr.title: Lorsque la détection varie selon les contextes : Context-as-Transform pour l'apprentissage tactile par peu d'exemples et augmentation de classes
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent Context-as-Transform FSCIL (CaT-FSCIL) pour résoudre l'apprentissage par peu d'exemples et augmentation de classes dans la détection tactile où les contextes d'acquisition variables dégradent les performances. La méthode décompose le contexte en composants structurés et résiduels, obtenant des résultats supérieurs sur les benchmarks de détection tactile.

de.title: Wenn die Erkennung je nach Kontext variiert: Context-as-Transform für taktiles Few-Shot-Klassen-Inkrementelles Lernen
de.summary: Forscher schlagen Context-as-Transform FSCIL (CaT-FSCIL) vor, um Few-Shot-Klassen-inkrementelles Lernen bei der taktilen Sensorik zu adressieren, wo unterschiedliche Erfassungskontexte die Leistung beeinträchtigen. Die Methode zerlegt den Kontext in strukturierte und Restkomponenten und erreicht überlegene Ergebnisse bei taktilen Sensorbenchmarks.

es.title: Cuando la detección varía según los contextos: Context-as-Transform para el aprendizaje táctico de pocas imágenes e incremento de clases
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen Context-as-Transform FSCIL (CaT-FSCIL) para abordar el aprendizaje táctico de pocas imágenes e incremento de clases en la detección táctil donde los contextos de adquisición variables degradan el rendimiento. El método descompone el contexto en componentes estructurados y residuales, logrando resultados superiores en benchmarks de detección táctil.

title: RubricEval: A Rubric-Level Meta-Evaluation Benchmark for LLM Judges in Instruction Following
summary: RubricEval introduces the first rubric-level meta-evaluation benchmark for instruction following with 3,486 quality-controlled instances, revealing that even GPT-4o achieves only 55.97% accuracy on the Hard subset. The study shows rubric-level evaluation outperforms checklist approaches and identifies common failure modes in LLM judging.
content:
arXiv:2603.25133v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Rubric-based evaluation has become a prevailing paradigm for evaluating instruction following in large language models (LLMs). Despite its widespread use, the reliability of these rubric-level evaluations remains unclear, calling for meta-evaluation. However, prior meta-evaluation efforts largely focus on the response level, failing to assess the fine-grained judgment accuracy that rubric-based evaluation relies on. To bridge this gap, we introduce RubricEval. Our benchmark features: (1) the first rubric-level meta-evaluation benchmark for instruction following, (2) diverse instructions and responses spanning multiple categories and model sources, and (3) a substantial set of 3,486 quality-controlled instances, along with Easy/Hard subsets that better differentiates judge performance. Our experiments reveal that rubric-level judging remains far from solved: even GPT-4o, a widely adopted judge in instruction-following benchmarks, achieves only 55.97% on Hard subset. Considering evaluation paradigm, rubric-level evaluation outperforms checklist-level, explicit reasoning improves accuracy, and both together reduce inter-judge variance. Through our established rubric taxonomy, we further identify common failure modes and offer actionable insights for reliable instruction-following evaluation.

zh.title: RubricEval：用于指令遵循中LLM评判的评分标准级元评估基准
zh.summary: RubricEval引入了首个用于指令遵循的评分标准级元评估基准，包含3,486个质量控制的实例，揭示即使GPT-4o在困难子集上的准确率也仅为55.97%。该研究表明评分标准级评估优于检查清单方法，并识别了LLM评判中的常见失败模式。

fr.title: RubricEval : Un benchmark de méta-évaluation au niveau du rubrique pour les juges LLM dans le suivi des instructions
fr.summary: RubricEval introduit le premier benchmark de méta-évaluation au niveau du rubrique pour le suivi des instructions avec 3 486 instances contrôlées en qualité, révélant que même GPT-4o n'obtient que 55,97% de précision sur le sous-ensemble Difficile. L'étude montre que l'évaluation au niveau du rubrique surpasse les approches par listes de contrôle et identifie les modes de défaillance courants dans le jugement des LLM.

de.title: RubricEval: Ein Rubrik-Level-Meta-Evaluierungs-Benchmark für LLM-Beurteiler bei der Befolgung von Anweisungen
de.summary: RubricEval führt den ersten Rubrik-Level-Meta-Evaluierungs-Benchmark für die Befolgung von Anweisungen mit 3.486 qualitätskontrollierten Instanzen ein und zeigt, dass selbst GPT-4o nur eine Genauigkeit von 55,97% auf der schwierigen Teilmenge erreicht. Die Studie zeigt, dass die Rubrik-Level-Evaluierung Checklisten-Ansätze überbietet und häufige Ausfallmuster bei der LLM-Beurteilung identifiziert.

es.title: RubricEval: Un benchmark de meta-evaluación a nivel de rúbrica para jueces LLM en el seguimiento de instrucciones
es.summary: RubricEval introduce el primer benchmark de meta-evaluación a nivel de rúbrica para el seguimiento de instrucciones con 3.486 instancias de calidad controlada, revelando que incluso GPT-4o logra solo 55,97% de precisión en el subconjunto difícil. El estudio muestra que la evaluación a nivel de rúbrica supera los enfoques de lista de verificación e identifica modos de fallo comunes en la evaluación de LLM.

title: UniAI-GraphRAG: Synergizing Ontology-Guided Extraction, Multi-Dimensional Clustering, and Dual-Channel Fusion for Robust Multi-Hop Reasoning
summary: UniAI-GraphRAG enhances open-source GraphRAG with ontology-guided knowledge extraction, multi-dimensional community clustering, and dual-channel graph retrieval fusion for improved multi-hop reasoning. Testing on MultiHopRAG benchmark demonstrates superior F1 scores compared to mainstream alternatives.
content:
arXiv:2603.25152v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems face significant challenges in complex reasoning, multi-hop queries, and domain-specific QA. While existing GraphRAG frameworks have made progress in structural knowledge organization, they still have limitations in cross-industry adaptability, community report integrity, and retrieval performance. This paper proposes UniAI-GraphRAG, an enhanced framework built upon open-source GraphRAG. The framework introduces three core innovations: (1) Ontology-Guided Knowledge Extraction that uses predefined Schema to guide LLMs in accurately identifying domain-specific entities and relations; (2) Multi-Dimensional Community Clustering Strategy that improves community completeness through alignment completion, attribute-based clustering, and multi-hop relationship clustering; (3) Dual-Channel Graph Retrieval Fusion that balances QA accuracy and performance through hybrid graph and community retrieval. Evaluation results on MultiHopRAG benchmark show that UniAI-GraphRAG outperforms mainstream open source solutions (e.g.LightRAG) in comprehensive F1 scores, particularly in inference and temporal queries. The code is available at https://github.com/UnicomAI/wanwu/tree/main/rag/rag_open_source/rag_core/graph.

zh.title: UniAI-GraphRAG：协同本体引导提取、多维聚类和双通道融合实现强大的多跳推理
zh.summary: UniAI-GraphRAG通过本体引导的知识提取、多维社群聚类和双通道图检索融合增强了开源GraphRAG，以改进多跳推理。在MultiHopRAG基准上的测试显示相比主流替代方案具有更高的F1分数。

fr.title: UniAI-GraphRAG : Synergisant l'extraction guidée par ontologie, le clustering multi-dimensionnel et la fusion à double canal pour un raisonnement multi-sauts robuste
fr.summary: UniAI-GraphRAG améliore GraphRAG open-source avec l'extraction de connaissances guidée par ontologie, le clustering communautaire multi-dimensionnel et la fusion de récupération de graphe à double canal pour un raisonnement multi-sauts amélioré. Les tests sur le benchmark MultiHopRAG démontrent des scores F1 supérieurs par rapport aux alternatives grand public.

de.title: UniAI-GraphRAG: Synergisierung von ontologiegesteuerter Extraktion, mehrdimensionalem Clustering und Dual-Channel-Fusion für robustes Multi-Hop-Reasoning
de.summary: UniAI-GraphRAG verbessert Open-Source-GraphRAG mit ontologiegesteuerter Wissenserkennung, mehrdimensionalem Community-Clustering und Dual-Channel-Graphen-Abruffusion für verbessertes Multi-Hop-Reasoning. Tests auf dem MultiHopRAG-Benchmark zeigen überlegene F1-Scores im Vergleich zu Mainstream-Alternativen.

es.title: UniAI-GraphRAG: Sinergizando extracción guiada por ontología, clustering multidimensional y fusión de doble canal para razonamiento robusto de múltiples saltos
es.summary: UniAI-GraphRAG mejora GraphRAG de código abierto con extracción de conocimiento guiada por ontología, clustering comunitario multidimensional y fusión de recuperación de gráficos de doble canal para un razonamiento de múltiples saltos mejorado. Las pruebas en el benchmark MultiHopRAG demuestran puntuaciones F1 superiores en comparación con alternativas convencionales.

title: Trace2Skill: Distill Trajectory-Local Lessons into Transferable Agent Skills
summary: Trace2Skill automates the generation of reusable skills for LLM agents by analyzing diverse execution trajectories through parallel analysis rather than sequential learning, demonstrating skills generated for smaller models transfer effectively to larger ones with up to 57.65% improvement. This addresses a critical bottleneck in scaling LLM agent capabilities while requiring no parameter updates.
content:
arXiv:2603.25158v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Equipping Large Language Model (LLM) agents with domain-specific skills is critical for tackling complex tasks. Yet, manual authoring creates a severe scalability bottleneck. Conversely, automated skill generation often yields fragile or fragmented results because it either relies on shallow parametric knowledge or sequentially overfits to non-generalizable trajectory-local lessons. To overcome this, we introduce Trace2Skill, a framework that mirrors how human experts author skills: by holistically analyzing broad execution experience before distilling it into a single, comprehensive guide. Instead of reacting sequentially to individual trajectories, Trace2Skill dispatches a parallel fleet of sub-agents to analyze a diverse pool of executions. It extracts trajectory-specific lessons and hierarchically consolidates them into a unified, conflict-free skill directory via inductive reasoning. Trace2Skill supports both deepening existing human-written skills and creating new ones from scratch. Experiments in challenging domains, such as spreadsheet, VisionQA and math reasoning, show that Trace2Skill significantly improves upon strong baselines, including Anthropic's official xlsx skills. Crucially, this trajectory-grounded evolution does not merely memorize task instances or model-specific quirks: evolved skills transfer across LLM scales and generalize to OOD settings. For example, skills evolved by Qwen3.5-35B on its own trajectories improved a Qwen3.5-122B agent by up to 57.65 absolute percentage points on WikiTableQuestions. Ultimately, our results demonstrate that complex agent experience can be packaged into highly transferable, declarative skills -- requiring no parameter updates, no external retrieval modules, and utilizing open-source models as small as 35B parameters.

zh.title: Trace2Skill：将轨迹局部经验提取为可转移的智能体技能
zh.summary: Trace2Skill通过并行分析而不是序列学习来分析多样化的执行轨迹，自动生成LLM智能体可重用的技能，演示为较小模型生成的技能能有效转移到较大模型，改进高达57.65%。这解决了扩展LLM智能体能力中的关键瓶颈，同时不需要参数更新。

fr.title: Trace2Skill : Distiller les leçons locales de trajectoire en compétences d'agent transférables
fr.summary: Trace2Skill automatise la génération de compétences réutilisables pour les agents LLM en analysant diverses trajectoires d'exécution par analyse parallèle plutôt que par apprentissage séquentiel, démontrant que les compétences générées pour les modèles plus petits se transfèrent efficacement aux modèles plus grands avec une amélioration jusqu'à 57,65%. Cela résout un goulot d'étranglement critique dans l'évolution des capacités des agents LLM sans nécessiter de mises à jour de paramètres.

de.title: Trace2Skill: Destillation von lokalen Trajektorielektionen in übertragbare Agent-Fähigkeiten
de.summary: Trace2Skill automatisiert die Generierung wiederverwendbarer Fähigkeiten für LLM-Agenten durch Analyse verschiedener Ausführungstrajektorien mittels paralleler Analyse statt sequenziellen Lernens und zeigt, dass für kleinere Modelle generierte Fähigkeiten effektiv auf größere Modelle mit einer Verbesserung von bis zu 57,65% übertragen werden. Dies behebt einen kritischen Engpass bei der Skalierung von LLM-Agent-Fähigkeiten ohne Parameter-Updates zu erfordern.

es.title: Trace2Skill: Destilar lecciones locales de trayectoria en habilidades de agente transferibles
es.summary: Trace2Skill automatiza la generación de habilidades reutilizables para agentes LLM analizando diversas trayectorias de ejecución mediante análisis paralelo en lugar de aprendizaje secuencial, demostrando que las habilidades generadas para modelos más pequeños se transfieren efectivamente a modelos más grandes con una mejora de hasta 57,65%. Esto aborda un cuello de botella crítico en el escalado de capacidades de agentes LLM sin requerir actualizaciones de parámetros.

title: The Competence Shadow: Theory and Bounds of AI Assistance in Safety Engineering
summary: The paper introduces a formal framework analyzing how AI assistance in safety engineering creates "competence shadows"—systematic blind spots in human reasoning that compound multiplicatively beyond simple predictions. The research demonstrates AI assistance effectiveness depends on workflow design rather than tool quality, arguing for workflow qualification over tool qualification for trustworthy Physical AI systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.25197v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As AI assistants become integrated into safety engineering workflows for Physical AI systems, a critical question emerges: does AI assistance improve safety analysis quality, or introduce systematic blind spots that surface only through post-deployment incidents? This paper develops a formal framework for AI assistance in safety analysis. We first establish why safety engineering resists benchmark-driven evaluation: safety competence is irreducibly multidimensional, constrained by context-dependent correctness, inherent incompleteness, and legitimate expert disagreement. We formalize this through a five-dimensional competence framework capturing domain knowledge, standards expertise, operational experience, contextual understanding, and judgment.
  We introduce the competence shadow: the systematic narrowing of human reasoning induced by AI-generated safety analysis. The shadow is not what the AI presents, but what it prevents from being considered. We formalize four canonical human-AI collaboration structures and derive closed-form performance bounds, demonstrating that the competence shadow compounds multiplicatively to produce degradation far exceeding naive additive estimates.
  The central finding is that AI assistance in safety engineering is a collaboration design problem, not a software procurement decision. The same tool degrades or improves analysis quality depending entirely on how it is used. We derive non-degradation conditions for shadow-resistant workflows and call for a shift from tool qualification toward workflow qualification for trustworthy Physical AI.

zh.title: 能力阴影：安全工程中AI协助的理论与界限
zh.summary: 该论文介绍了一个正式框架，分析AI协助在安全工程中如何创造"能力阴影"——系统性的人类推理盲点，其影响成倍增加，超过简单的预测。研究表明AI协助的有效性取决于工作流设计而不是工具质量，主张对可信任的物理AI系统进行工作流资格认证而不是工具资格认证。

fr.title: L'Ombre de Compétence : Théorie et Limites de l'Assistance IA en Ingénierie de Sécurité
fr.summary: L'article introduit un cadre formel analysant comment l'assistance IA en ingénierie de sécurité crée des « ombres de compétence » — des points aveugles systématiques dans le raisonnement humain qui se composent de manière multiplicative au-delà des simples prédictions. La recherche démontre que l'efficacité de l'assistance IA dépend de la conception du flux de travail plutôt que de la qualité de l'outil, plaidant pour la qualification du flux de travail plutôt que la qualification de l'outil pour les systèmes d'IA physique dignes de confiance.

de.title: Der Kompetenzschatten: Theorie und Grenzen der KI-Unterstützung in der Sicherheitstechnik
de.summary: Das Papier führt einen formalen Rahmen ein, der analysiert, wie KI-Unterstützung in der Sicherheitstechnik „Kompetenzsschatten" schafft — systematische Blindstellen in menschlichem Denken, die sich multiplikativ über einfache Vorhersagen hinaus verstärken. Die Forschung zeigt, dass die Effektivität der KI-Unterstützung von der Workflow-Gestaltung und nicht von der Werkzeugqualität abhängt und argumentiert für die Workflow-Qualifizierung statt Werkzeug-Qualifizierung bei vertrauenswürdigen Physical-AI-Systemen.

es.title: La Sombra de Competencia: Teoría y Límites de la Asistencia de IA en Ingeniería de Seguridad
es.summary: El artículo introduce un marco formal que analiza cómo la asistencia de IA en ingeniería de seguridad crea «sombras de competencia» — puntos ciegos sistemáticos en el razonamiento humano que se componen de manera multiplicativa más allá de simples predicciones. La investigación demuestra que la efectividad de la asistencia de IA depende del diseño del flujo de trabajo en lugar de la calidad de la herramienta, argumentando a favor de la calificación del flujo de trabajo en lugar de la calificación de la herramienta para sistemas de IA física confiables.

title: Probabilistic Abstract Interpretation on Neural Networks via Grids Approximation
summary: This work applies probabilistic abstract interpretation theory to neural networks to analyze density distribution flow across infinite input spaces without testing every input. The framework uses different abstract domains and mathematical transformers to approximate neural network behavior, providing theoretical tools for analyzing real-world problems computationally.
content:
arXiv:2603.25266v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Probabilistic abstract interpretation is a theory used to extract particular properties of a computer program when it is infeasible to test every single inputs. In this paper we apply the theory on neural networks for the same purpose: to analyse density distribution flow of all possible inputs of a neural network when a network has uncountably many or countable but infinitely many inputs. We show how this theoretical framework works in neural networks and then discuss different abstract domains and corresponding Moore-Penrose pseudo-inverses together with abstract transformers used in the framework. We also present experimental examples to show how this framework helps to analyse real world problems.

zh.title: 通过网格近似的神经网络概率抽象解释
zh.summary: 这项工作将概率抽象解释理论应用于神经网络，以分析跨越无限输入空间的密度分布流，无需测试每个输入。该框架使用不同的抽象域和数学变换器来近似神经网络行为，为计算分析现实世界问题提供理论工具。

fr.title: Interprétation Abstraite Probabiliste sur les Réseaux de Neurones via l'Approximation par Grilles
fr.summary: Ce travail applique la théorie de l'interprétation abstraite probabiliste aux réseaux de neurones pour analyser le flux de distribution de densité à travers des espaces d'entrée infinis sans tester chaque entrée. Le cadre utilise différents domaines abstraits et transformateurs mathématiques pour approximer le comportement des réseaux de neurones, fournissant des outils théoriques pour analyser les problèmes du monde réel sur le plan informatique.

de.title: Probabilistische Abstrakte Interpretation auf neuronalen Netzen durch Gitterannäherung
de.summary: Diese Arbeit wendet die Theorie der probabilistischen abstrakten Interpretation auf neuronale Netze an, um den Dichteververteilungsfluss über unendliche Eingaberäume zu analysieren, ohne jeden Eingang zu testen. Das Framework verwendet verschiedene abstrakte Domänen und mathematische Transformatoren, um das Verhalten neuronaler Netze zu approximieren und stellt theoretische Werkzeuge zur rechnergestützten Analyse von realen Problemen bereit.

es.title: Interpretación Abstracta Probabilística en Redes Neuronales mediante Aproximación por Cuadrículas
es.summary: Este trabajo aplica la teoría de interpretación abstracta probabilística a redes neuronales para analizar el flujo de distribución de densidad en espacios de entrada infinitos sin probar cada entrada. El marco utiliza diferentes dominios abstractos y transformadores matemáticos para aproximar el comportamiento de redes neuronales, proporcionando herramientas teóricas para analizar problemas del mundo real computacionalmente.

title: Distribution and Clusters Approximations as Abstract Domains in Probabilistic Abstract Interpretation to Neural Network Analysis
summary: The paper extends probabilistic abstract interpretation for neural networks by introducing distribution approximation and clusters approximation methods as novel abstract domains. These alternatives to grids approximation provide different approaches for analyzing neural network density distribution flow with corresponding theoretical transformers.
content:
arXiv:2603.25273v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The probabilistic abstract interpretation framework of neural network analysis analyzes a neural network by analyzing its density distribution flow of all possible inputs. The grids approximation is one of abstract domains the framework uses which abstracts concrete space into grids. In this paper, we introduce two novel approximation methods: distribution approximation and clusters approximation. We show how these two methods work in theory with corresponding abstract transformers with help of illustrations of some simple examples.

zh.title: 分布和集群近似作为神经网络分析中概率抽象解释的抽象域
zh.summary: 该论文通过引入分布近似和集群近似方法作为新颖的抽象域，扩展了神经网络的概率抽象解释。这些网格近似的替代方案提供了不同的方法来分析神经网络密度分布流，具有相应的理论变换器。

fr.title: Approximations de Distribution et de Clusters comme Domaines Abstraits dans l'Interprétation Abstraite Probabiliste pour l'Analyse des Réseaux de Neurones
fr.summary: L'article étend l'interprétation abstraite probabiliste pour les réseaux de neurones en introduisant les méthodes d'approximation de distribution et d'approximation de clusters comme nouveaux domaines abstraits. Ces alternatives à l'approximation par grilles fournissent différentes approches pour analyser le flux de distribution de densité des réseaux de neurones avec les transformateurs théoriques correspondants.

de.title: Verteilungs- und Cluster-Approximationen als abstrakte Domänen in der probabilistischen abstrakten Interpretation zur Analyse neuronaler Netze
de.summary: Das Papier erweitert die probabilistische abstrakte Interpretation für neuronale Netze, indem Verteilungsannäherung und Cluster-Approximationsmethoden als neue abstrakte Domänen eingeführt werden. Diese Alternativen zur Gitterannäherung bieten verschiedene Ansätze zur Analyse des Dichteververteilungsflusses neuronaler Netze mit entsprechenden theoretischen Transformatoren.

es.title: Aproximaciones de Distribución y Clusters como Dominios Abstractos en Interpretación Abstracta Probabilística para Análisis de Redes Neuronales
es.summary: El artículo extiende la interpretación abstracta probabilística para redes neuronales introduciendo métodos de aproximación de distribución y aproximación de clusters como nuevos dominios abstractos. Estas alternativas a la aproximación por cuadrículas proporcionan diferentes enfoques para analizar el flujo de distribución de densidad de redes neuronales con transformadores teóricos correspondientes.

title: A Gait Foundation Model Predicts Multi-System Health Phenotypes from 3D Skeletal Motion
summary: Researchers developed a foundation model analyzing 3D skeletal motion from over 3,400 individuals that successfully predicts multiple health metrics including age, BMI, and visceral fat, establishing gait as a systemic biomarker. Different body regions encode distinct health information, with legs predicting metabolic patterns while torso indicates sleep and lifestyle characteristics, enabling passive consumer-grade health monitoring.
content:
arXiv:2603.25283v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Gait is increasingly recognized as a vital sign, yet current approaches treat it as a symptom of specific pathologies rather than a systemic biomarker. We developed a gait foundation model for 3D skeletal motion from 3,414 deeply phenotyped adults, recorded via a depth camera during five motor tasks. Learned embeddings outperformed engineered features, predicting age (Pearson r = 0.69), BMI (r = 0.90), and visceral adipose tissue area (r = 0.82). Embeddings significantly predicted 1,980 of 3,210 phenotypic targets; after adjustment for age, BMI, VAT, and height, gait provided independent gains in all 18 body systems in males and 17 of 18 in females, and improved prediction of clinical diagnoses and medication use. Anatomical ablation revealed that legs dominated metabolic and frailty predictions while torso encoded sleep and lifestyle phenotypes. These findings establish gait as an independent multi-system biosignal, motivating translation to consumer-grade video and its integration as a scalable, passive vital sign.

zh.title: 步态基础模型从3D骨骼运动预测多系统健康表型
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了一个基础模型，分析来自超过3,400个人的3D骨骼运动，成功预测了包括年龄、BMI和内脏脂肪在内的多个健康指标，建立了步态作为系统生物标志物。不同的身体部位编码不同的健康信息，腿部预测代谢模式，而躯干表示睡眠和生活方式特征，实现被动的消费级健康监测。

fr.title: Un Modèle Fondateur de Marche Prédit les Phénotypes de Santé Multi-Systémique à partir du Mouvement Squelettique 3D
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont développé un modèle fondateur analysant les mouvements squelettiques 3D de plus de 3 400 individus qui prédisent avec succès plusieurs métriques de santé, notamment l'âge, l'IMC et la graisse viscérale, établissant la marche comme un biomarqueur systémique. Différentes régions du corps encodent des informations de santé distinctes, les jambes prédisant les modèles métaboliques tandis que le torse indique les caractéristiques du sommeil et du mode de vie, permettant un suivi passif de la santé au niveau des consommateurs.

de.title: Ein Gangmodell-Fundament sagt multiSystemische Gesundheitsphenotypen aus 3D-Skelettbewegung voraus
de.summary: Forscher entwickelten ein Grundmodell, das 3D-Skelettbewegungen von über 3.400 Personen analysiert und erfolgreich mehrere Gesundheitsmetriken wie Alter, BMI und viszerales Fett vorhersagt und etabliert Gangart als systemischen Biomarker. Verschiedene Körperregionen kodieren unterschiedliche Gesundheitsinformationen, wobei die Beine Stoffwechselmuster vorhersagen, während der Rumpf Schlaf- und Lebensstileigenschaften anzeigt und passive Gesundheitsüberwachung auf Verbraucherebene ermöglicht.

es.title: Un Modelo Fundamental de Marcha Predice Fenotipos de Salud Multisistémica a partir del Movimiento Esquelético 3D
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollaron un modelo fundamental que analiza el movimiento esquelético 3D de más de 3,400 individuos que predice con éxito múltiples métricas de salud, incluida edad, IMC y grasa visceral, estableciendo la marcha como un biomarcador sistémico. Diferentes regiones del cuerpo codifican información de salud distinta, con las piernas prediciendo patrones metabólicos mientras que el torso indica características del sueño y estilo de vida, permitiendo monitoreo pasivo de salud a nivel de consumidor.

title: SliderQuant: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for LLMs
summary: SliderQuant introduces a post-training quantization framework that adapts quantization intensity across different LLM layers rather than treating them equally, addressing higher sensitivity in shallow and deep layers. Extensive experiments show the method outperforms existing quantization approaches across multiple model families while maintaining competitive performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.25284v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In this paper, we address post-training quantization (PTQ) for large language models (LLMs) from an overlooked perspective: given a pre-trained high-precision LLM, the predominant sequential quantization framework treats different layers equally, but this may be not optimal in challenging bit-width settings. We empirically study the quantization impact of different layers on model accuracy, and observe that: (1) shallow/deep layers are usually more sensitive to quantization than intermediate layers; (2) among shallow/deep layers, the most sensitive one is the first/last layer, which exhibits significantly larger quantization error than others. These empirical observations imply that the quantization design for different layers of LLMs is required on multiple levels instead of a single level shared to all layers. Motivated by this, we propose a new PTQ framework termed Sliding-layer Quantization (SliderQuant) that relies on a simple adaptive sliding quantization concept facilitated by few learnable parameters. The base component of SliderQuant is called inter-layer sliding quantization, which incorporates three types of novel sliding window designs tailored for addressing the varying quantization sensitivity of shallow, intermediate and deep layers. The other component is called intra-layer sliding quantization that leverages an incremental strategy to quantize each window. As a result, SliderQuant has a strong ability to reduce quantization errors across layers. Extensive experiments on basic language generation, zero-shot commonsense reasoning and challenging math and code tasks with various LLMs, including Llama/Llama2/Llama3/Qwen2.5 model families, DeepSeek-R1 distilled models and large MoE models, show that our method outperforms existing PTQ methods (including the latest PTQ methods using rotation transformations) for both weight-only quantization and weight-activation quantization.

zh.title: SliderQuant：大型语言模型的精确后训练量化
zh.summary: SliderQuant引入了一个后训练量化框架，该框架在不同的LLM层之间调整量化强度，而不是平等对待，解决了浅层和深层的更高敏感性。广泛的实验表明该方法在多个模型族中超过了现有的量化方法，同时保持了有竞争力的性能。

fr.title: SliderQuant : Quantification post-entraînement précise pour les LLM
fr.summary: SliderQuant introduit un framework de quantification post-entraînement qui adapte l'intensité de quantification à travers différentes couches de LLM plutôt que de les traiter uniformément, résolvant la sensibilité plus élevée dans les couches peu profondes et profondes. Des expériences extensives montrent que la méthode surpasse les approches de quantification existantes dans plusieurs familles de modèles tout en maintenant des performances compétitives.

de.title: SliderQuant: Genaue Nachtraining-Quantisierung für LLMs
de.summary: SliderQuant führt ein Nachtraining-Quantisierungs-Framework ein, das die Quantisierungsintensität über verschiedene LLM-Schichten hinweg anpasst, anstatt sie gleich zu behandeln, und adressiert damit die höhere Empfindlichkeit in flachen und tiefen Schichten. Umfangreiche Experimente zeigen, dass die Methode bestehende Quantisierungsansätze über mehrere Modellfamilien hinweg übertrifft und gleichzeitig wettbewerbsfähige Leistungen beibehält.

es.title: SliderQuant: Cuantización Post-Entrenamiento Precisa para LLMs
es.summary: SliderQuant introduce un framework de cuantización post-entrenamiento que adapta la intensidad de cuantización en diferentes capas de LLM en lugar de tratarlas por igual, abordando la sensibilidad más alta en capas superficiales y profundas. Experimentos extensos muestran que el método supera los enfoques de cuantización existentes en múltiples familias de modelos mientras mantiene un rendimiento competitivo.

title: DAGverse: Building Document-Grounded Semantic DAGs from Scientific Papers
summary: DAGverse automatically extracts semantic Directed Acyclic Graphs from scientific papers by leveraging explicit DAG figures as supervision signals and grounding structures in accompanying text evidence. The released DAGverse-1 dataset of 108 expert-validated semantic DAGs demonstrates superior performance to existing Vision-Language Models for document-grounded reasoning tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.25293v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) are widely used to represent structured knowledge in scientific and technical domains. However, datasets for real-world DAGs remain scarce because constructing them typically requires expert interpretation of domain documents. We study Doc2SemDAG construction: recovering a preferred semantic DAG from a document together with the cited evidence and context that explain it. This problem is challenging because a document may admit multiple plausible abstractions, the intended structure is often implicit, and the supporting evidence is scattered across prose, equations, captions, and figures. To address these challenges, we leverage scientific papers containing explicit DAG figures as a natural source of supervision. In this setting, the DAG figure provides the DAG structure, while the accompanying text provides context and explanation. We introduce DAGverse, a framework for constructing document-grounded semantic DAGs from online scientific papers. Its core component, DAGverse-Pipeline, is a semi-automatic system designed to produce high-precision semantic DAG examples through figure classification, graph reconstruction, semantic grounding, and validation. As a case study, we test the framework for causal DAGs and release DAGverse-1, a dataset of 108 expert-validated semantic DAGs with graph-level, node-level, and edge-level evidence. Experiments show that DAGverse-Pipeline outperforms existing Vision-Language Models on DAG classification and annotation. DAGverse provides a foundation for document-grounded DAG benchmarks and opens new directions for studying structured reasoning grounded in real-world evidence.

zh.title: DAGverse：从科学论文中构建文档基础语义有向无环图
zh.summary: DAGverse通过利用显式DAG图作为监督信号和伴随文本证据中的接地结构，从科学论文中自动提取语义有向无环图。发布的DAGverse-1数据集包含108个专家验证的语义有向无环图，在文档基础推理任务上的表现超过了现有的视觉语言模型。

fr.title: DAGverse : Construction de DAGs sémantiques ancrés dans les documents à partir d'articles scientifiques
fr.summary: DAGverse extrait automatiquement des Graphes Acycliques Dirigés sémantiques à partir d'articles scientifiques en exploitant les figures DAG explicites comme signaux de supervision et les structures ancrées dans les preuves textuelles accompagnantes. L'ensemble de données DAGverse-1 libéré, contenant 108 DAGs sémantiques validés par des experts, démontre des performances supérieures aux modèles Vision-Langage existants pour les tâches de raisonnement ancré dans les documents.

de.title: DAGverse: Erstellung dokumentengestützter semantischer DAGs aus wissenschaftlichen Arbeiten
de.summary: DAGverse extrahiert automatisch semantische gerichtete azyklische Graphen aus wissenschaftlichen Arbeiten, indem explizite DAG-Abbildungen als Überwachungssignale und verankernde Strukturen in begleitenden Textbeweisen genutzt werden. Der veröffentlichte DAGverse-1-Datensatz mit 108 von Experten validierten semantischen DAGs demonstriert überlegene Leistungen gegenüber bestehenden Vision-Language-Modellen für dokumentengestützte Reasoning-Aufgaben.

es.title: DAGverse: Construcción de DAGs Semánticos Anclados en Documentos desde Artículos Científicos
es.summary: DAGverse extrae automáticamente Gráficos Acíclicos Dirigidos semánticos de artículos científicos aprovechando figuras DAG explícitas como señales de supervisión y estructuras ancladas en evidencia textual acompañante. El conjunto de datos DAGverse-1 liberado, con 108 DAGs semánticos validados por expertos, demuestra un rendimiento superior a los Modelos Vision-Lenguaje existentes para tareas de razonamiento anclado en documentos.

title: Evaluating Language Models for Harmful Manipulation
summary: A framework evaluating AI manipulation through context-specific human-AI interaction studies with over 10,000 participants found language models can produce manipulative behaviors and induce belief and behavior changes, with effectiveness varying significantly by domain and geography. The research emphasizes manipulation evaluation must be context-specific rather than universal.
content:
arXiv:2603.25326v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Interest in the concept of AI-driven harmful manipulation is growing, yet current approaches to evaluating it are limited. This paper introduces a framework for evaluating harmful AI manipulation via context-specific human-AI interaction studies. We illustrate the utility of this framework by assessing an AI model with 10,101 participants spanning interactions in three AI use domains (public policy, finance, and health) and three locales (US, UK, and India). Overall, we find that that the tested model can produce manipulative behaviours when prompted to do so and, in experimental settings, is able to induce belief and behaviour changes in study participants. We further find that context matters: AI manipulation differs between domains, suggesting that it needs to be evaluated in the high-stakes context(s) in which an AI system is likely to be used. We also identify significant differences across our tested geographies, suggesting that AI manipulation results from one geographic region may not generalise to others. Finally, we find that the frequency of manipulative behaviours (propensity) of an AI model is not consistently predictive of the likelihood of manipulative success (efficacy), underscoring the importance of studying these dimensions separately. To facilitate adoption of our evaluation framework, we detail our testing protocols and make relevant materials publicly available. We conclude by discussing open challenges in evaluating harmful manipulation by AI models.

zh.title: 评估语言模型的有害操纵
zh.summary: 一个框架通过包含超过10,000名参与者的上下文特定的人工智能交互研究来评估AI操纵，发现语言模型可以产生操纵行为并诱导信念和行为改变，其有效性在不同领域和地区有显著差异。该研究强调操纵评估必须是上下文特定的，而不是通用的。

fr.title: Évaluation des modèles de langage pour la manipulation nuisible
fr.summary: Un framework évaluant la manipulation par l'IA à travers des études d'interaction humain-IA spécifiques au contexte avec plus de 10 000 participants a constaté que les modèles de langage peuvent produire des comportements manipulateurs et induire des changements de croyances et de comportements, avec une efficacité variant significativement selon le domaine et la géographie. La recherche souligne que l'évaluation de la manipulation doit être spécifique au contexte plutôt qu'universelle.

de.title: Bewertung von Sprachmodellen für schädliche Manipulation
de.summary: Ein Framework zur Bewertung von KI-Manipulation durch kontextspezifische Mensch-KI-Interaktionsstudien mit über 10.000 Teilnehmern zeigte, dass Sprachmodelle manipulatives Verhalten erzeugen und Überzeugungs- und Verhaltensänderungen hervorrufen können, wobei die Wirksamkeit nach Domäne und Geografie erheblich variiert. Die Forschung betont, dass die Evaluierung von Manipulation kontextspezifisch sein muss, nicht universell.

es.title: Evaluación de Modelos de Lenguaje para Manipulación Dañina
es.summary: Un framework evaluando la manipulación de IA a través de estudios de interacción humano-IA específicos del contexto con más de 10,000 participantes encontró que los modelos de lenguaje pueden producir comportamientos manipuladores e inducir cambios de creencias y comportamiento, con la efectividad variando significativamente por dominio y geografía. La investigación enfatiza que la evaluación de manipulación debe ser específica del contexto en lugar de universal.

title: Macroscopic Characteristics of Mixed Traffic Flow with Deep Reinforcement Learning Based Automated and Human-Driven Vehicles
summary: Analysis of Deep Reinforcement Learning-controlled autonomous vehicles in mixed traffic found that transitioning from fully human-driven to fully RL-controlled traffic increases road capacity by 7.52% and improves fuel efficiency by up to 28.98%. The study demonstrates RL-based AVs enhance traffic performance while maintaining safety, supporting deployment of learning-based autonomous vehicle control.
content:
arXiv:2603.25328v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Automated Vehicle (AV) control in mixed traffic, where AVs coexist with human-driven vehicles, poses significant challenges in balancing safety, efficiency, comfort, fuel efficiency, and compliance with traffic rules while capturing heterogeneous driver behavior. Traditional car-following models, such as the Intelligent Driver Model (IDM), often struggle to generalize across diverse traffic scenarios and typically do not account for fuel efficiency, motivating the use of learning-based approaches. Although Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) has shown strong microscopic performance in car-following conditions, its macroscopic traffic flow characteristics remain underexplored. This study focuses on analyzing the macroscopic traffic flow characteristics and fuel efficiency of DRL-based models in mixed traffic. A Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) algorithm is implemented for AVs' control and trained using the NGSIM highway dataset, enabling realistic interaction with human-driven vehicles. Traffic performance is evaluated using the Fundamental Diagram (FD) under varying driver heterogeneity, heterogeneous time-gap penetration levels, and different shares of RL-controlled vehicles. A macroscopic level comparison of fuel efficiency between the RL-based AV model and the IDM is also conducted. Results show that traffic performance is sensitive to the distribution of safe time gaps and the proportion of RL vehicles. Transitioning from fully human-driven to fully RL-controlled traffic can increase road capacity by approximately 7.52%. Further, RL-based AVs also improve average fuel efficiency by about 28.98% at higher speeds (above 50 km/h), and by 1.86% at lower speeds (below 50 km/h) compared to the IDM. Overall, the DRL framework enhances traffic capacity and fuel efficiency without compromising safety.

zh.title: 深度强化学习驱动的自动驾驶和人类驾驶车辆混合交通流的宏观特性
zh.summary: 对深度强化学习控制的自动驾驶车辆在混合交通中的分析表明，从完全人类驾驶向完全强化学习控制交通的转变可将道路容量提高7.52%，燃油效率提高最多28.98%。该研究表明基于强化学习的自动驾驶车辆在保持安全的同时增强了交通性能，支持基于学习的自动驾驶控制的部署。

fr.title: Caractéristiques Macroscopiques du Flux de Trafic Mixte avec Véhicules Autonomes et Conducteurs Humains Basés sur l'Apprentissage par Renforcement Profond
fr.summary: L'analyse des véhicules autonomes contrôlés par apprentissage par renforcement profond dans un trafic mixte a révélé que la transition d'un trafic entièrement conduit par l'homme à un trafic entièrement contrôlé par RL augmente la capacité routière de 7,52% et améliore l'efficacité énergétique jusqu'à 28,98%. L'étude démontre que les véhicules autonomes basés sur RL améliorent les performances du trafic tout en maintenant la sécurité, soutenant le déploiement du contrôle des véhicules autonomes basé sur l'apprentissage.

de.title: Makroskopische Charakteristiken des gemischten Verkehrsflusses mit tiefem Verstärkungslerngesteuerten automatisierten und menschlich gesteuerten Fahrzeugen
de.summary: Die Analyse von durch tiefes Verstärkenlernen gesteuerten autonomen Fahrzeugen in gemischtem Verkehr zeigte, dass der Übergang von vollständig menschlich gesteuert zu vollständig RL-gesteuert die Straßenkapazität um 7,52% erhöht und die Kraftstoffeffizienz um bis zu 28,98% verbessert. Die Studie zeigt, dass RL-basierte automatisierte Fahrzeuge die Verkehrsleistung verbessern, während sie die Sicherheit beibehalten, und unterstützt die Bereitstellung von lernbasierten Fahrzeugsteuerungen.

es.title: Características Macroscópicas del Flujo de Tráfico Mixto con Vehículos Autónomos y Conducidos por Humanos Basados en Aprendizaje Profundo por Refuerzo
es.summary: El análisis de vehículos autónomos controlados por aprendizaje por refuerzo profundo en tráfico mixto encontró que la transición de tráfico completamente conducido por humanos a tráfico completamente controlado por RL aumenta la capacidad vial en un 7,52% y mejora la eficiencia de combustible hasta un 28,98%. El estudio demuestra que los vehículos autónomos basados en RL mejoran el rendimiento del tráfico mientras mantienen la seguridad, apoyando el despliegue del control de vehículos autónomos basado en aprendizaje.

title: Agentic Trust Coordination for Federated Learning through Adaptive Thresholding and Autonomous Decision Making in Sustainable and Resilient Industrial Networks
summary: The paper presents an agentic trust coordination mechanism for federated learning in industrial networks using a server-side control loop to dynamically respond to trust-related signals rather than fixed parameters. This framework improves federated learning reliability in resource-constrained environments with faulty or adversarial updates without modifying client-side training.
content:
arXiv:2603.25334v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Distributed intelligence in industrial networks increasingly integrates sensing, communication, and computation across heterogeneous and resource constrained devices. Federated learning (FL) enables collaborative model training in such environments, but its reliability is affected by inconsistent client behaviour, noisy sensing conditions, and the presence of faulty or adversarial updates. Trust based mechanisms are commonly used to mitigate these effects, yet most remain statistical and heuristic, relying on fixed parameters or simple adaptive rules that struggle to accommodate changing operating conditions.
  This paper presents a lightweight agentic trust coordination approach for FL in sustainable and resilient industrial networks. The proposed Agentic Trust Control Layer operates as a server side control loop that observes trust related and system level signals, interprets their evolution over time, and applies targeted trust adjustments when instability is detected. The approach extends prior adaptive trust mechanisms by enabling context aware intervention decisions, rather than relying on fixed or purely reactive parameter updates. By explicitly separating observation, reasoning, and action, the proposed framework supports stable FL operation without modifying client side training or increasing communication overhead.

zh.title: 通过自适应阈值处理和自主决策在可持续和具有弹性的工业网络中进行联合学习的代理信任协调
zh.summary: 该论文提出了一种用于工业网络中联合学习的代理信任协调机制，使用服务器端控制环路动态响应信任相关信号，而不是固定参数。该框架在资源受限的环境中改进了联合学习的可靠性，处理有缺陷或对抗性的更新，而无需修改客户端训练。

fr.title: Coordination de Confiance Agentique pour l'Apprentissage Fédéré par Seuillage Adaptatif et Prise de Décision Autonome dans des Réseaux Industriels Durables et Résilients
fr.summary: L'article présente un mécanisme de coordination de confiance agentique pour l'apprentissage fédéré dans les réseaux industriels utilisant une boucle de contrôle côté serveur pour répondre dynamiquement aux signaux liés à la confiance plutôt qu'à des paramètres fixes. Ce cadre améliore la fiabilité de l'apprentissage fédéré dans les environnements à ressources limitées avec des mises à jour défectueuses ou adversariales sans modifier l'entraînement côté client.

de.title: Agentenbasierte Vertrauenskoordination für föderiertes Lernen durch adaptive Schwellenwertbestimmung und autonome Entscheidungsfindung in nachhaltigen und widerstandsfähigen Industrienetzwerken
de.summary: Das Papier präsentiert einen agentengestützten Vertrauenskoordinationsmechanismus für föderiertes Lernen in Industrienetzwerken unter Verwendung einer serverseitigen Regelschleife, um dynamisch auf vertrauensbezogene Signale anstelle fester Parameter zu reagieren. Dieses Framework verbessert die Zuverlässigkeit des föderierten Lernens in ressourcenbeschränkten Umgebungen mit fehlerhaften oder gegnerischen Updates, ohne das Training auf der Client-Seite zu ändern.

es.title: Coordinación de Confianza Agencial para Aprendizaje Federado a través de Umbralización Adaptativa y Toma de Decisiones Autónoma en Redes Industriales Sostenibles y Resilientes
es.summary: El artículo presenta un mecanismo de coordinación de confianza agencial para aprendizaje federado en redes industriales utilizando un bucle de control del lado del servidor para responder dinámicamente a señales relacionadas con la confianza en lugar de parámetros fijos. Este marco mejora la confiabilidad del aprendizaje federado en entornos con recursos limitados con actualizaciones defectuosas o adversariales sin modificar el entrenamiento del lado del cliente.

title: 4OPS: Structural Difficulty Modeling in Integer Arithmetic Puzzles
summary: Researchers analyzed structural determinants of difficulty in integer arithmetic puzzles using a dynamic-programming solver, creating a dataset of 3.4 million labeled instances. The study found difficulty is fully determined by interpretable structural attributes, particularly input value count, enabling explainable difficulty estimation for adaptive learning systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.25356v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Arithmetic puzzle games provide a controlled setting for studying difficulty in mathematical reasoning tasks, a core challenge in adaptive learning systems. We investigate the structural determinants of difficulty in a class of integer arithmetic puzzles inspired by number games. We formalize the problem and develop an exact dynamic-programming solver that enumerates reachable targets, extracts minimal-operation witnesses, and enables large-scale labeling.
  Using this solver, we construct a dataset of over 3.4 million instances and define difficulty via the minimum number of operations required to reach a target. We analyze the relationship between difficulty and solver-derived features. While baseline machine learning models based on bag- and target-level statistics can partially predict solvability, they fail to reliably distinguish easy instances. In contrast, we show that difficulty is fully determined by a small set of interpretable structural attributes derived from exact witnesses. In particular, the number of input values used in a minimal construction serves as a minimal sufficient statistic for difficulty under this labeling.
  These results provide a transparent, computationally grounded account of puzzle difficulty that bridges symbolic reasoning and data-driven modeling. The framework supports explainable difficulty estimation and principled task sequencing, with direct implications for adaptive arithmetic learning and intelligent practice systems.

zh.title: 4OPS：整数算术谜题的结构难度建模
zh.summary: 研究人员使用动态规划求解器分析了整数算术谜题难度的结构决定因素，创建了340万个标记实例的数据集。该研究发现难度完全由可解释的结构属性决定，特别是输入值的数量，使得自适应学习系统能够进行可解释的难度估计。

fr.title: 4OPS : Modélisation de la Difficulté Structurelle dans les Énigmes d'Arithmétique Entière
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont analysé les déterminants structurels de la difficulté dans les énigmes d'arithmétique entière en utilisant un solveur de programmation dynamique, créant un ensemble de données de 3,4 millions d'instances étiquetées. L'étude a révélé que la difficulté est entièrement déterminée par des attributs structurels interprétables, en particulier le nombre de valeurs d'entrée, permettant une estimation de difficulté explicable pour les systèmes d'apprentissage adaptatif.

de.title: 4OPS: Strukturelle Schwierigkeitsmodellierung bei ganzzahligen arithmetischen Rätseln
de.summary: Forscher analysierten strukturelle Bestimmungsfaktoren der Schwierigkeit bei ganzzahligen arithmetischen Rätseln unter Verwendung eines dynamischen Programmierungslösers und erstellten einen Datensatz mit 3,4 Millionen gekennzeichneten Instanzen. Die Studie ergab, dass die Schwierigkeit vollständig durch interpretierbare strukturelle Attribute bestimmt wird, besonders durch die Anzahl der Eingabewerte, und ermöglicht so erklärbare Schwierigkeitsschätzung für adaptive Lernsysteme.

es.title: 4OPS: Modelado de Dificultad Estructural en Acertijos de Aritmética Entera
es.summary: Los investigadores analizaron los determinantes estructurales de la dificultad en acertijos de aritmética entera utilizando un solucionador de programación dinámica, creando un conjunto de datos de 3,4 millones de instancias etiquetadas. El estudio encontró que la dificultad está completamente determinada por atributos estructurales interpretables, particularmente la cantidad de valores de entrada, permitiendo la estimación explicable de dificultad para sistemas de aprendizaje adaptativo.

title: Does Structured Intent Representation Generalize? A Cross-Language, Cross-Model Empirical Study of 5W3H Prompting
summary: A comprehensive empirical study evaluates whether 5W3H structured prompting generalizes across three languages and three LLMs, finding AI-assisted automatic expansion achieves comparable alignment to manual crafting. Structured prompts reduce cross-model variance and eliminate systematic biases present in unstructured prompts, improving accessibility and consistency for non-expert users.
content:
arXiv:2603.25379v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Does structured intent representation generalize across languages and models? We study PPS (Prompt Protocol Specification), a 5W3H-based framework for structured intent representation in human-AI interaction, and extend prior Chinese-only evidence along three dimensions: two additional languages (English and Japanese), a fourth condition in which a user's simple prompt is automatically expanded into a full 5W3H specification by an AI-assisted authoring interface, and a new research question on cross-model output consistency. Across 2,160 model outputs (3 languages x 4 conditions x 3 LLMs x 60 tasks), we find that AI-expanded 5W3H prompts (Condition D) show no statistically significant difference in goal alignment from manually crafted 5W3H prompts (Condition C) across all three languages, while requiring only a single-sentence input from the user. Structured PPS conditions often reduce or reshape cross-model output variance, though this effect is not uniform across languages and metrics; the strongest evidence comes from identifying spurious low variance in unconstrained baselines. We also show that unstructured prompts exhibit a systematic dual-inflation bias: artificially high composite scores and artificially low apparent cross-model variance. These findings suggest that structured 5W3H representations can improve intent alignment and accessibility across languages and models, especially when AI-assisted authoring lowers the barrier for non-expert users.

zh.title: 结构化意图表示是否可以泛化?5W3H提示的跨语言、跨模型实证研究
zh.summary: 一项全面的实证研究评估了5W3H结构化提示是否能在三种语言和三个大型语言模型上进行泛化,发现人工智能辅助的自动扩展达到与手动创建相当的对齐效果。结构化提示减少了跨模型方差,消除了非结构化提示中存在的系统偏差,提高了非专业用户的可访问性和一致性。

fr.title: La représentation structurée des intentions se généralise-t-elle ? Une étude empirique multilingue et multi-modèles d'invite 5W3H
fr.summary: Une étude empirique complète évalue si l'invite structurée 5W3H se généralise sur trois langues et trois modèles de langage, constatant que l'expansion automatique assistée par l'IA atteint un alignement comparable à la création manuelle. Les invites structurées réduisent la variance entre modèles et éliminent les biais systématiques présents dans les invites non structurées, améliorant l'accessibilité et la cohérence pour les utilisateurs non experts.

de.title: Lässt sich die strukturierte Intentionsdarstellung verallgemeinern? Eine sprachübergreifende und modellübergreifende empirische Studie zum 5W3H-Prompting
de.summary: Eine umfassende empirische Studie bewertet, ob die strukturierte 5W3H-Eingabeaufforderung über drei Sprachen und drei große Sprachmodelle hinweg verallgemeinert werden kann, und stellt fest, dass KI-gestützte automatische Erweiterung eine vergleichbare Ausrichtung mit manuellem Erstellen erreicht. Strukturierte Aufforderungen reduzieren die modellübergreifende Varianz und beseitigen systematische Verzerrungen in unstrukturierten Aufforderungen, was die Zugänglichkeit und Konsistenz für nicht-Experten verbessert.

es.title: ¿Se generaliza la representación estructurada de intenciones? Un estudio empírico multilingüe y multimodelo del aviso 5W3H
es.summary: Un estudio empírico exhaustivo evalúa si el aviso estructurado 5W3H se generaliza en tres idiomas y tres modelos de lenguaje grande, encontrando que la expansión automática asistida por IA logra una alineación comparable a la creación manual. Los avisos estructurados reducen la varianza entre modelos y eliminan los sesgos sistemáticos presentes en los avisos no estructurados, mejorando la accesibilidad y consistencia para usuarios no expertos.

title: Beyond Content Safety: Real-Time Monitoring for Reasoning Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models
summary: This paper identifies reasoning safety as a critical security dimension distinct from content safety, proposing a taxonomy of nine categories of unsafe reasoning behaviors in chain-of-thought processes. The authors introduce a Reasoning Safety Monitor achieving up to 84.88% accuracy in detecting unsafe reasoning steps, establishing reasoning-level monitoring as essential for safely deploying complex language models.
content:
arXiv:2603.25412v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly rely on explicit chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning to solve complex tasks, yet the safety of the reasoning process itself remains largely unaddressed. Existing work on LLM safety focuses on content safety--detecting harmful, biased, or factually incorrect outputs -- and treats the reasoning chain as an opaque intermediate artifact. We identify reasoning safety as an orthogonal and equally critical security dimension: the requirement that a model's reasoning trajectory be logically consistent, computationally efficient, and resistant to adversarial manipulation. We make three contributions. First, we formally define reasoning safety and introduce a nine-category taxonomy of unsafe reasoning behaviors, covering input parsing errors, reasoning execution errors, and process management errors. Second, we conduct a large-scale prevalence study annotating 4111 reasoning chains from both natural reasoning benchmarks and four adversarial attack methods (reasoning hijacking and denial-of-service), confirming that all nine error types occur in practice and that each attack induces a mechanistically interpretable signature. Third, we propose a Reasoning Safety Monitor: an external LLM-based component that runs in parallel with the target model, inspects each reasoning step in real time via a taxonomy-embedded prompt, and dispatches an interrupt signal upon detecting unsafe behavior. Evaluation on a 450-chain static benchmark shows that our monitor achieves up to 84.88\% step-level localization accuracy and 85.37\% error-type classification accuracy, outperforming hallucination detectors and process reward model baselines by substantial margins. These results demonstrate that reasoning-level monitoring is both necessary and practically achievable, and establish reasoning safety as a foundational concern for the secure deployment of large reasoning models.

zh.title: 超越内容安全:大型语言模型推理漏洞的实时监测
zh.summary: 本文将推理安全识别为一个关键的安全维度,与内容安全不同,提出了链式思维过程中不安全推理行为的九个类别的分类法。作者引入了推理安全监测器,在检测不安全推理步骤方面达到84.88%的准确率,确立了推理级别的监测对于安全部署复杂语言模型至关重要。

fr.title: Au-delà de la sécurité du contenu: surveillance en temps réel des vulnérabilités de raisonnement dans les grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: Cet article identifie la sécurité du raisonnement comme une dimension de sécurité critique distincte de la sécurité du contenu, proposant une taxonomie de neuf catégories de comportements de raisonnement non sécuritaires dans les processus de chaîne de pensée. Les auteurs introduisent un Moniteur de Sécurité du Raisonnement atteignant une précision de 84,88 % dans la détection des étapes de raisonnement non sécuritaires, établissant la surveillance au niveau du raisonnement comme essentielle pour le déploiement sécurisé de modèles de langage complexes.

de.title: Jenseits der Inhaltssicherheit: Echtzeitüberwachung von Sicherheitslücken beim Denken in großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Dieses Papier identifiziert Reasoning-Sicherheit als eine kritische Sicherheitsdimension, die sich von der Inhaltssicherheit unterscheidet, und schlägt eine Taxonomie von neun Kategorien unsicherer Denkverhalten in Chain-of-Thought-Prozessen vor. Die Autoren führen einen Reasoning Safety Monitor ein, der eine Genauigkeit von bis zu 84,88 % beim Erkennen unsicherer Denkschritte erreicht, und stellen fest, dass die Überwachung auf Reasoning-Ebene für die sichere Bereitstellung komplexer Sprachmodelle unerlässlich ist.

es.title: Más allá de la seguridad del contenido: supervisión en tiempo real de vulnerabilidades de razonamiento en modelos de lenguaje grande
