es.summary: Este documento identifica la seguridad del razonamiento como una dimensión de seguridad crítica distinta de la seguridad del contenido, proponiendo una taxonomía de nueve categorías de comportamientos de razonamiento inseguro en procesos de cadena de pensamiento. Los autores introducen un Monitor de Seguridad del Razonamiento que logra una precisión de hasta el 84,88% en la detección de pasos de razonamiento inseguro, estableciendo la supervisión a nivel de razonamiento como esencial para el despliegue seguro de modelos de lenguaje complejos.

title: Modernising Reinforcement Learning-Based Navigation for Embodied Semantic Scene Graph Generation
summary: Researchers modernized reinforcement learning for embodied scene graph generation by testing modern optimization algorithms and fine-grained action formulations, demonstrating 21% relative improvement in scene graph completeness. This work advances autonomous exploration strategies for embodied agents constructing structured world models under resource constraints.
content:
arXiv:2603.25415v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Semantic world models enable embodied agents to reason about objects, relations, and spatial context beyond purely geometric representations. In Organic Computing, such models are a key enabler for objective-driven self-adaptation under uncertainty and resource constraints. The core challenge is to acquire observations maximising model quality and downstream usefulness within a limited action budget.
  Semantic scene graphs (SSGs) provide a structured and compact representation for this purpose. However, constructing them within a finite action horizon requires exploration strategies that trade off information gain against navigation cost and decide when additional actions yield diminishing returns.
  This work presents a modular navigation component for Embodied Semantic Scene Graph Generation and modernises its decision-making by replacing the policy-optimisation method and revisiting the discrete action formulation. We study compact and finer-grained, larger discrete motion sets and compare a single-head policy over atomic actions with a factorised multi-head policy over action components. We evaluate curriculum learning and optional depth-based collision supervision, and assess SSG completeness, execution safety, and navigation behaviour.
  Results show that replacing the optimisation algorithm alone improves SSG completeness by 21\% relative to the baseline under identical reward shaping. Depth mainly affects execution safety (collision-free motion), while completeness remains largely unchanged. Combining modern optimisation with a finer-grained, factorised action representation yields the strongest overall completeness--efficiency trade-off.

zh.title: 现代化具身语义场景图生成的强化学习导航
zh.summary: 研究人员通过测试现代优化算法和细粒度动作公式化,使具身场景图生成的强化学习现代化,展示了场景图完整性的21%相对改进。这项工作推进了具身代理在资源受限条件下构建结构化世界模型的自主探索策略。

fr.title: Moderniser la navigation basée sur l'apprentissage par renforcement pour la génération de graphiques de scènes sémantiques incarnés
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont modernisé l'apprentissage par renforcement pour la génération de graphiques de scènes incarnés en testant des algorithmes d'optimisation modernes et des formulations d'actions à grains fins, démontrant une amélioration relative de 21 % en termes de complétude des graphiques de scènes. Ce travail fait progresser les stratégies d'exploration autonome pour les agents incarnés construisant des modèles de monde structurés dans le cadre de contraintes de ressources.

de.title: Modernisierung der verstärkungslerngestützten Navigation für die Generierung von verkörperten semantischen Szenengraphen
de.summary: Forscher modernisierten Reinforcement Learning für die Generierung von Szenengraphen durch das Testen moderner Optimierungsalgorithmen und feingranularer Aktionsformulierungen, was eine 21-prozentige relative Verbesserung der Szenengraph-Vollständigkeit demonstriert. Diese Arbeit schreitet autonome Explorationsstrategien für verkörperte Agenten voran, die strukturierte Weltmodelle unter Ressourcenbeschränkungen konstruieren.

es.title: Modernización de la navegación basada en aprendizaje reforzado para la generación de gráficos de escenas semánticas corporificadas
es.summary: Los investigadores modernizaron el aprendizaje reforzado para la generación de gráficos de escenas corporificadas mediante la prueba de algoritmos de optimización modernos y formulaciones de acciones de grano fino, demostrando una mejora relativa del 21% en la integridad de los gráficos de escenas. Este trabajo avanza las estrategias de exploración autónoma para agentes corporificados que construyen modelos de mundo estructurados bajo restricciones de recursos.

title: Cross-Model Disagreement as a Label-Free Correctness Signal
summary: This paper introduces cross-model disagreement—measuring how a second model responds to a first model's output—as a training-free method to detect when LLMs are wrong without ground truth labels. It addresses the critical problem of 'confident errors' where models are certain but incorrect, achieving 0.75 AUROC on MMLU compared to 0.59 for traditional uncertainty baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.25450v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Detecting when a language model is wrong without ground truth labels is a fundamental challenge for safe deployment. Existing approaches rely on a model's own uncertainty -- such as token entropy or confidence scores -- but these signals fail critically on the most dangerous failure mode: confident errors, where a model is wrong but certain. In this work we introduce cross-model disagreement as a correctness indicator -- a simple, training-free signal that can be dropped into existing production systems, routing pipelines, and deployment monitoring infrastructure without modification. Given a model's generated answer, cross-model disagreement computes how surprised or uncertain a second verifier model is when reading that answer via a single forward pass. No generation from the verifying model is required, and no correctness labels are needed. We instantiate this principle as Cross-Model Perplexity (CMP), which measures the verifying model's surprise at the generating model's answer tokens, and Cross-Model Entropy (CME), which measures the verifying model's uncertainty at those positions. Both CMP and CME outperform within-model uncertainty baselines across benchmarks spanning reasoning, retrieval, and mathematical problem solving (MMLU, TriviaQA, and GSM8K). On MMLU, CMP achieves a mean AUROC of 0.75 against a within-model entropy baseline of 0.59. These results establish cross-model disagreement as a practical, training-free approach to label-free correctness estimation, with direct applications in deployment monitoring, model routing, selective prediction, data filtering, and scalable oversight of production language model systems.

zh.title: 跨模型分歧作为无标签正确性信号
zh.summary: 本文介绍了跨模型分歧作为无需训练的方法来检测大语言模型错误，无需真值标签。它解决了"自信错误"的关键问题，其中模型确定但不正确，在MMLU上达到0.75 AUROC，相比传统不确定性基线的0.59。

fr.title: Désaccord entre modèles comme signal de correction sans étiquette
fr.summary: Cet article introduit le désaccord entre modèles comme une méthode sans entraînement pour détecter quand les LLM se trompent sans étiquettes de vérité terrain. Il traite du problème critique des « erreurs confiantes » où les modèles sont certains mais incorrects, atteignant 0,75 AUROC sur MMLU par rapport à 0,59 pour les lignes de base d'incertitude traditionnelles.

de.title: Modellübergreifende Unstimmigkeit als kennzeichnungsfreies Korrektheitssignal
de.summary: Dieses Papier führt modellübergreifende Unstimmigkeit als trainingsfreie Methode ein, um zu erkennen, wenn LLMs falsch sind, ohne Grundwahrheitsetiketten. Es befasst sich mit dem kritischen Problem der „vertrauensvollen Fehler", bei denen Modelle sicher, aber falsch sind, und erreicht 0,75 AUROC auf MMLU im Vergleich zu 0,59 für traditionelle Unsicherheitsbaselines.

es.title: Desacuerdo entre modelos como señal de corrección sin etiqueta
es.summary: Este artículo introduce el desacuerdo entre modelos como un método sin entrenamiento para detectar cuándo los LLM se equivocan sin etiquetas de verdad fundamental. Aborda el problema crítico de los "errores confiados" donde los modelos son ciertos pero incorrectos, logrando 0.75 AUROC en MMLU en comparación con 0.59 para las líneas de base de incertidumbre tradicionales.

title: Retraining as Approximate Bayesian Inference
summary: This work reframes model retraining as approximate Bayesian inference, introducing the concept of 'learning debt'—the gap between continuously updated beliefs and frozen deployed models. It proposes a decision-theoretic framework that generates evidence-based retraining triggers rather than relying on calendar schedules.
content:
arXiv:2603.25480v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Model retraining is usually treated as an ongoing maintenance task. But as Harrison Katz now argues, retraining can be better understood as approximate Bayesian inference under computational constraints. The gap between a continuously updated belief state and your frozen deployed model is "learning debt," and the retraining decision is a cost minimization problem with a threshold that falls out of your loss function. In this article Katz provides a decision-theoretic framework for retraining policies. The result is evidence-based triggers that replace calendar schedules and make governance auditable. For readers less familiar with the Bayesian and decision-theoretic language, key terms are defined in a glossary at the end of the article.

zh.title: 重新训练作为近似贝叶斯推理
zh.summary: 这项工作将模型重新训练重新定义为近似贝叶斯推理，引入"学习债"的概念——连续更新的信念和冻结部署模型之间的差距。它提出了一个决策论框架，生成基于证据的重新训练触发器，而不是依赖日历计划。

fr.title: Réentraînement en tant qu'inférence bayésienne approximative
fr.summary: Ce travail réinterprète le réentraînement du modèle comme une inférence bayésienne approximative, introduisant le concept de « dette d'apprentissage »—l'écart entre les croyances continuellement mises à jour et les modèles déployés figés. Il propose un cadre théorique décisionnel qui génère des déclencheurs de réentraînement basés sur des preuves plutôt que de s'appuyer sur des calendriers.

de.title: Umschulung als ungefähre Bayesische Inferenz
de.summary: Diese Arbeit interpretiert das Modelltraining neu als ungefähre Bayesische Inferenz und führt das Konzept der „Lernschuld" ein—die Lücke zwischen kontinuierlich aktualisierten Überzeugungen und eingefrorenen bereitgestellten Modellen. Sie schlägt einen entscheidungstheoretischen Rahmen vor, der evidenzgestützte Umschulungsauslöser generiert, anstatt sich auf Kalender zu verlassen.

es.title: Reentrenamiento como inferencia bayesiana aproximada
es.summary: Este trabajo reinterpreta el reentrenamiento del modelo como inferencia bayesiana aproximada, introduciendo el concepto de "deuda de aprendizaje"—la brecha entre las creencias continuamente actualizadas y los modelos implementados congelados. Propone un marco teórico de decisión que genera desencadenantes de reentrenamiento basados en evidencia en lugar de depender de calendarios.

title: EcoThink: A Green Adaptive Inference Framework for Sustainable and Accessible Agents
summary: EcoThink is an energy-aware adaptive inference framework that dynamically routes queries to skip unnecessary reasoning steps like Chain-of-Thought for simple tasks. It reduces inference energy consumption by 40.4% on average without statistically significant performance loss, addressing sustainability concerns in LLM deployment.
content:
arXiv:2603.25498v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As the Web transitions from static retrieval to generative interaction, the escalating environmental footprint of Large Language Models (LLMs) presents a critical sustainability challenge. Current paradigms indiscriminately apply computation-intensive strategies like Chain-of-Thought (CoT) to billions of daily queries, causing LLM overthinking, a redundancy that amplifies carbon emissions and operational barriers. This inefficiency directly undermines UN Sustainable Development Goals 13 (Climate Action) and 10 (Reduced Inequalities) by hindering equitable AI access in resource-constrained regions. To address this, we introduce EcoThink, an energy-aware adaptive inference framework designed to reconcile high-performance AI intelligence with environmental responsibility. EcoThink employs a lightweight, distillation-based router to dynamically assess query complexity, skipping unnecessary reasoning for factoid retrieval while reserving deep computation for complex logic. Extensive evaluations across 9 diverse benchmarks demonstrate that EcoThink reduces inference energy by 40.4% on average (up to 81.9% for web knowledge retrieval) without statistically significant performance loss. By mitigating algorithmic waste, EcoThink offers a scalable path toward a sustainable, inclusive, and energy-efficient generative AI Agent.

zh.title: EcoThink：可持续和可访问智能体的绿色自适应推理框架
zh.summary: EcoThink是一个能量感知的自适应推理框架，动态路由查询以跳过不必要的推理步骤，例如对简单任务进行思维链推理。它平均减少推理能耗40.4%，没有统计上显著的性能损失，解决了大语言模型部署中的可持续性问题。

fr.title: EcoThink : Un cadre d'inférence adaptative écologique pour des agents durables et accessibles
fr.summary: EcoThink est un cadre d'inférence adaptative conscient de l'énergie qui achemine dynamiquement les requêtes pour ignorer les étapes de raisonnement inutiles comme la chaîne de pensée pour les tâches simples. Il réduit la consommation d'énergie d'inférence de 40,4% en moyenne sans perte de performance statistiquement significative, abordant les préoccupations de durabilité dans le déploiement des LLM.

de.title: EcoThink: Ein grünes adaptives Inferenzframework für nachhaltige und zugängliche Agenten
de.summary: EcoThink ist ein energiegewahrtes adaptives Inferenzframework, das Abfragen dynamisch leitet, um unnötige Denkschritte wie Chain-of-Thought bei einfachen Aufgaben zu überspringen. Es reduziert den Energieverbrauch der Inferenz um durchschnittlich 40,4% ohne statistisch signifikanten Leistungsverlust und befasst sich mit Nachhaltigkeitsbedenken bei der LLM-Bereitstellung.

es.title: EcoThink: Un marco de inferencia adaptativa verde para agentes sostenibles y accesibles
es.summary: EcoThink es un marco de inferencia adaptativa consciente de la energía que enruta dinámicamente consultas para omitir pasos de razonamiento innecesarios como Chain-of-Thought para tareas simples. Reduce el consumo de energía de inferencia en un promedio de 40.4% sin pérdida de rendimiento estadísticamente significativa, abordando preocupaciones de sostenibilidad en la implementación de LLM.

title: Voxtral TTS
summary: Voxtral TTS is an expressive multilingual text-to-speech model that clones voices from just 3 seconds of reference audio using a hybrid architecture combining auto-regressive and flow-matching generation. In human evaluations, it outperforms ElevenLabs Flash v2.5 with a 68.4% win rate for naturalness and expressivity.
content:
arXiv:2603.25551v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce Voxtral TTS, an expressive multilingual text-to-speech model that generates natural speech from as little as 3 seconds of reference audio. Voxtral TTS adopts a hybrid architecture that combines auto-regressive generation of semantic speech tokens with flow-matching for acoustic tokens. These tokens are encoded and decoded with Voxtral Codec, a speech tokenizer trained from scratch with a hybrid VQ-FSQ quantization scheme. In human evaluations conducted by native speakers, Voxtral TTS is preferred for multilingual voice cloning due to its naturalness and expressivity, achieving a 68.4\% win rate over ElevenLabs Flash v2.5. We release the model weights under a CC BY-NC license.

zh.title: Voxtral文本转语音
zh.summary: Voxtral TTS是一个富有表现力的多语言文本转语音模型，可以从仅3秒的参考音频中克隆声音，使用混合架构结合自回归和流匹配生成。在人工评估中，它以68.4%的胜率在自然性和表现力上超过ElevenLabs Flash v2.5。

fr.title: Voxtral TTS
fr.summary: Voxtral TTS est un modèle de synthèse vocale multilingue expressif qui clone des voix à partir de seulement 3 secondes d'audio de référence en utilisant une architecture hybride combinant la génération auto-régressive et par appariement de flux. Dans les évaluations humaines, il surpasse ElevenLabs Flash v2.5 avec un taux de victoire de 68,4% pour le naturel et l'expressivité.

de.title: Voxtral TTS
de.summary: Voxtral TTS ist ein ausdrucksstarkes mehrsprachiges Text-zu-Sprache-Modell, das Stimmen aus nur 3 Sekunden Referenzaudio mit einer hybriden Architektur klont, die autoregressives und Flow-Matching-Generierung kombiniert. Bei menschlichen Bewertungen übertrifft es ElevenLabs Flash v2.5 mit einer Erfolgsquote von 68,4% für Natürlichkeit und Ausdruckskraft.

es.title: Voxtral TTS
es.summary: Voxtral TTS es un modelo de síntesis de voz multilingüe expresivo que clona voces a partir de solo 3 segundos de audio de referencia usando una arquitectura híbrida que combina generación autorregresiva y de emparejamiento de flujo. En evaluaciones humanas, supera a ElevenLabs Flash v2.5 con una tasa de victoria del 68,4% en naturalidad y expresividad.

title: Is Mathematical Problem-Solving Expertise in Large Language Models Associated with Assessment Performance?
summary: This study examines whether stronger math problem-solving ability in LLMs correlates with stronger step-level assessment performance on GSM8K and MATH benchmarks. Results show that models achieve higher assessment accuracy on problems they solve correctly, though assessing solutions remains more difficult than solving original problems.
content:
arXiv:2603.25633v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used in math education not only as problem solvers but also as assessors of learners' reasoning. However, it remains unclear whether stronger math problem-solving ability is associated with stronger step-level assessment performance. This study examines that relationship using the GSM8K and MATH subsets of PROCESSBENCH, a human-annotated benchmark for identifying the earliest erroneous step in mathematical reasoning. We evaluate two LLM-based math tutor agent settings, instantiated with GPT-4 and GPT-5, in two independent tasks on the same math problems: solving the original problem and assessing a benchmark-provided solution by predicting the earliest erroneous step. Results show a consistent within-model pattern: assessment accuracy is substantially higher on math problem items the same model solved correctly than on items it solved incorrectly, with statistically significant associations across both models and datasets. At the same time, assessment remains more difficult than direct problem solving, especially on error-present solutions. These findings suggest that math problem-solving expertise supports stronger assessment performance, but reliable step-level diagnosis also requires additional capabilities such as step tracking, monitoring, and precise error localization. The results have implications for the design and evaluation of AI-supported Adaptive Instructional Systems (AISs) for formative assessment in math education.

zh.title: 大型语言模型中的数学问题求解专业知识是否与评估性能相关？
zh.summary: 本研究考察了LLMs中更强的数学问题求解能力是否与GSM8K和MATH基准上的步骤级评估性能相关。结果表明，模型在正确解决的问题上达到更高的评估准确性，但评估解决方案比解决原始问题更困难。

fr.title: L'expertise en résolution de problèmes mathématiques dans les grands modèles de langage est-elle associée aux performances d'évaluation ?
fr.summary: Cette étude examine si une meilleure capacité de résolution de problèmes mathématiques dans les LLM corrèle avec une meilleure performance d'évaluation au niveau des étapes sur les repères GSM8K et MATH. Les résultats montrent que les modèles obtiennent une plus grande précision d'évaluation sur les problèmes qu'ils résolvent correctement, bien que l'évaluation des solutions reste plus difficile que la résolution des problèmes originaux.

de.title: Korreliert die Expertise zur Lösung mathematischer Probleme in großen Sprachmodellen mit der Bewertungsleistung?
de.summary: Diese Studie untersucht, ob eine stärkere mathematische Problemlösungsfähigkeit in LLMs mit einer stärkeren schrittweisen Bewertungsleistung auf den GSM8K- und MATH-Benchmarks korreliert. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass Modelle eine höhere Bewertungsgenauigkeit bei Problemen erreichen, die sie korrekt lösen, obwohl die Bewertung von Lösungen schwieriger bleibt als die Lösung ursprünglicher Probleme.

es.title: ¿Está la experiencia en resolución de problemas matemáticos en modelos de lenguaje grandes asociada con el desempeño de evaluación?
es.summary: Este estudio examina si una capacidad más fuerte de resolución de problemas matemáticos en LLM se correlaciona con un mejor desempeño de evaluación a nivel de pasos en los criterios de referencia GSM8K y MATH. Los resultados muestran que los modelos logran una mayor precisión de evaluación en problemas que resuelven correctamente, aunque evaluar soluciones sigue siendo más difícil que resolver problemas originales.

title: Agent Factories for High Level Synthesis: How Far Can General-Purpose Coding Agents Go in Hardware Optimization?
summary: This paper presents agent factories—a two-stage pipeline using multiple autonomous coding agents to optimize hardware designs from high-level specifications. Testing on diverse kernels achieves a mean 8.27x speedup over baseline with scaling to 10 agents, demonstrating that general-purpose AI agents can effectively apply known hardware optimization patterns.
content:
arXiv:2603.25719v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present an empirical study of how far general-purpose coding agents -- without hardware-specific training -- can optimize hardware designs from high-level algorithmic specifications. We introduce an agent factory, a two-stage pipeline that constructs and coordinates multiple autonomous optimization agents.
  In Stage~1, the pipeline decomposes a design into sub-kernels, independently optimizes each using pragma and code-level transformations, and formulates an Integer Linear Program (ILP) to assemble globally promising configurations under an area constraint. In Stage~2, it launches $N$ expert agents over the top ILP solutions, each exploring cross-function optimizations such as pragma recombination, loop fusion, and memory restructuring that are not captured by sub-kernel decomposition.
  We evaluate the approach on 12 kernels from HLS-Eval and Rodinia-HLS using Claude Code (Opus~4.5/4.6) with AMD Vitis HLS. Scaling from 1 to 10 agents yields a mean $8.27\times$ speedup over baseline, with larger gains on harder benchmarks: streamcluster exceeds $20\times$ and kmeans reaches approximately $10\times$. Across benchmarks, agents consistently rediscover known hardware optimization patterns without domain-specific training, and the best designs often do not originate from top-ranked ILP candidates, indicating that global optimization exposes improvements missed by sub-kernel search. These results establish agent scaling as a practical and effective axis for HLS optimization.

zh.title: 用于高层综合的代理工厂：通用编码代理在硬件优化中能走多远？
zh.summary: 本文提出了代理工厂——一个两阶段流程，使用多个自主编码代理从高层规范优化硬件设计。在多样化核心上的测试实现了相对基线8.27倍的平均加速，扩展到10个代理，表明通用AI代理能够有效应用已知的硬件优化模式。

fr.title: Usines d'agents pour la synthèse de haut niveau : Jusqu'où les agents de codage à usage général peuvent-ils aller dans l'optimisation matérielle ?
fr.summary: Cet article présente les usines d'agents — un pipeline en deux étapes utilisant plusieurs agents de codage autonomes pour optimiser les conceptions matérielles à partir de spécifications de haut niveau. Le test sur des noyaux diversifiés atteint une accélération moyenne de 8,27x par rapport à la ligne de base avec une mise à l'échelle à 10 agents, démontrant que les agents IA à usage général peuvent appliquer efficacement les modèles d'optimisation matérielle connus.

de.title: Agent Factories für High-Level-Synthese: Wie weit können universelle Codierungsagenten bei der Hardwareoptimierung gehen?
de.summary: Dieses Papier präsentiert Agent Factories – eine zweistufige Pipeline, die mehrere autonome Coding-Agenten zur Optimierung von Hardware-Designs aus High-Level-Spezifikationen nutzt. Tests an verschiedenen Kerneln erreichen eine durchschnittliche Speedup von 8,27x gegenüber der Baseline mit Skalierung auf 10 Agenten, was zeigt, dass universelle KI-Agenten bekannte Hardware-Optimierungsmuster effektiv anwenden können.

es.title: Fábricas de agentes para síntesis de alto nivel: ¿Hasta dónde pueden llegar los agentes de codificación de propósito general en la optimización de hardware?
es.summary: Este artículo presenta fábricas de agentes, un pipeline de dos etapas que utiliza múltiples agentes de codificación autónomos para optimizar diseños de hardware a partir de especificaciones de alto nivel. Las pruebas en diversos núcleos logran una aceleración promedio de 8.27x sobre la línea base con escalado a 10 agentes, demostrando que los agentes de IA de propósito general pueden aplicar efectivamente patrones conocidos de optimización de hardware.

title: R-C2: Cycle-Consistent Reinforcement Learning Improves Multimodal Reasoning
summary: R-C2 introduces a reinforcement learning framework that enforces cross-modal cycle consistency to resolve conflicts between visual and textual model representations. This approach improves multimodal reasoning accuracy by up to 7.6 points by using internal inconsistencies as a label-free learning signal.
content:
arXiv:2603.25720v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Robust perception and reasoning require consistency across sensory modalities. Yet current multimodal models often violate this principle, yielding contradictory predictions for visual and textual representations of the same concept. Rather than masking these failures with standard voting mechanisms, which can amplify systematic biases, we show that cross-modal inconsistency provides a rich and natural signal for learning. We introduce RC2, a reinforcement learning framework that resolves internal conflicts by enforcing cross-modal cycle consistency. By requiring a model to perform backward inference, switch modalities, and reliably reconstruct the answer through forward inference, we obtain a dense, label-free reward. This cyclic constraint encourages the model to align its internal representations autonomously. Optimizing for this structure mitigates modality-specific errors and improves reasoning accuracy by up to 7.6 points. Our results suggest that advanced reasoning emerges not only from scaling data, but also from enforcing a structurally consistent understanding of the world.

zh.title: R-C2：循环一致强化学习改进多模态推理
zh.summary: R-C2引入了一个强化学习框架，通过强制跨模态循环一致性来解决视觉和文本模型表示之间的冲突。该方法通过使用内部不一致作为无标签学习信号，将多模态推理精度提高了7.6个百分点。

fr.title: R-C2 : L'apprentissage par renforcement cohérent en cycle améliore le raisonnement multimodal
fr.summary: R-C2 introduit un cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement qui applique la cohérence cyclique inter-modale pour résoudre les conflits entre les représentations des modèles visuels et textuels. Cette approche améliore la précision du raisonnement multimodal jusqu'à 7,6 points en utilisant les incohérences internes comme signal d'apprentissage sans étiquette.

de.title: R-C2: Zykluskonsistentes Reinforcement Learning verbessert multimodale Argumentation
de.summary: R-C2 führt ein Reinforcement-Learning-Framework ein, das zyklische Cross-Modal-Konsistenz erzwingt, um Konflikte zwischen visuellen und textlichen Modelldarstellungen zu lösen. Dieser Ansatz verbessert die Genauigkeit des multimodalen Denkens um bis zu 7,6 Punkte, indem interne Inkonsistenzen als labelfreies Lernsignal verwendet werden.

es.title: R-C2: El aprendizaje por refuerzo consistente en ciclos mejora el razonamiento multimodal
es.summary: R-C2 introduce un marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo que aplica la consistencia cíclica entre modalidades para resolver conflictos entre las representaciones de modelos visuales y textuales. Este enfoque mejora la precisión del razonamiento multimodal hasta en 7,6 puntos al utilizar inconsistencias internas como una señal de aprendizaje sin etiquetas.

title: Back to Basics: Revisiting ASR in the Age of Voice Agents
summary: WildASR is a multilingual diagnostic benchmark that isolates automatic speech recognition failures along three axes: environmental degradation, demographic shift, and linguistic diversity. It reveals that ASR systems exhibit severe performance degradation in real-world conditions and often hallucinate plausible content, posing safety risks for voice agents.
content:
arXiv:2603.25727v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems have achieved near-human accuracy on curated benchmarks, yet still fail in real-world voice agents under conditions that current evaluations do not systematically cover. Without diagnostic tools that isolate specific failure factors, practitioners cannot anticipate which conditions, in which languages, will cause what degree of degradation. We introduce WildASR, a multilingual (four-language) diagnostic benchmark sourced entirely from real human speech that factorizes ASR robustness along three axes: environmental degradation, demographic shift, and linguistic diversity. Evaluating seven widely used ASR systems, we find severe and uneven performance degradation, and model robustness does not transfer across languages or conditions. Critically, models often hallucinate plausible but unspoken content under partial or degraded inputs, creating concrete safety risks for downstream agent behavior. Our results demonstrate that targeted, factor-isolated evaluation is essential for understanding and improving ASR reliability in production systems. Besides the benchmark itself, we also present three analytical tools that practitioners can use to guide deployment decisions.

zh.title: 回归基础：在语音代理时代重新审视自动语音识别
zh.summary: WildASR是一个多语言诊断基准，沿着三个轴隔离自动语音识别失败：环境恶化、人口变化和语言多样性。它揭示了ASR系统在真实条件下表现出严重的性能退化，并经常产生看似合理的幻觉内容，对语音代理构成安全风险。

fr.title: Retour aux bases : Revisiter la reconnaissance automatique de la parole à l'ère des agents vocaux
fr.summary: WildASR est un benchmark de diagnostic multilingue qui isole les défaillances de la reconnaissance automatique de la parole selon trois axes : dégradation environnementale, évolution démographique et diversité linguistique. Il révèle que les systèmes ASR présentent une dégradation sévère des performances en conditions réelles et hallucinent souvent du contenu plausible, posant des risques de sécurité pour les agents vocaux.

de.title: Zurück zu den Grundlagen: ASR im Zeitalter von Sprachagenten überdenken
de.summary: WildASR ist eine mehrsprachige Diagnose-Benchmark, die Fehler der automatischen Spracherkennung entlang dreier Achsen isoliert: Umweltbeeinträchtigung, demografische Verschiebung und sprachliche Vielfalt. Sie zeigt, dass ASR-Systeme unter realen Bedingungen schwere Leistungsabbau aufweisen und häufig plausible Inhalte halluzinieren, was Sicherheitsrisiken für Sprachagenten darstellt.

es.title: Volviendo a lo básico: Revisitando el reconocimiento automático de voz en la era de los agentes de voz
es.summary: WildASR es un benchmark diagnóstico multilingüe que aísla las fallas del reconocimiento automático de voz a lo largo de tres ejes: degradación ambiental, cambio demográfico y diversidad lingüística. Revela que los sistemas ASR exhiben una degradación severa del rendimiento en condiciones del mundo real e frecuentemente alucinar contenido plausible, lo que presenta riesgos de seguridad para agentes de voz.

title: Training the Knowledge Base through Evidence Distillation and Write-Back Enrichment
summary: WriteBack-RAG treats the knowledge base in retrieval-augmented generation as a trainable component, using labeled examples to identify and distill relevant information into compact knowledge units. Evaluation across four RAG methods and six benchmarks shows consistent improvements averaging +2.14% with knowledge benefits transferring across different RAG pipelines.
content:
arXiv:2603.25737v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The knowledge base in a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system is typically assembled once and never revised, even though the facts a query requires are often fragmented across documents and buried in irrelevant content. We argue that the knowledge base should be treated as a trainable component and propose WriteBack-RAG, a framework that uses labeled examples to identify where retrieval succeeds, isolate the relevant documents, and distill them into compact knowledge units that are indexed alongside the original corpus. Because the method modifies only the corpus, it can be applied once as an offline preprocessing step and combined with any RAG pipeline. Across four RAG methods, six benchmarks, and two LLM backbones, WriteBack-RAG improves every evaluated setting, with gains averaging +2.14%. Cross-method transfer experiments further show that the distilled knowledge benefits RAG pipelines other than the one used to produce it, confirming that the improvement resides in the corpus itself.

zh.title: 通过证据蒸馏和写回富集训练知识库
zh.summary: WriteBack-RAG将检索增强生成中的知识库视为可训练的组件，使用标记示例来识别相关信息并将其蒸馏成紧凑的知识单元。在四种RAG方法和六个基准的评估中显示一致的改进，平均提高2.14%，知识优势在不同的RAG管道间转移。

fr.title: Former la base de connaissances par distillation des preuves et enrichissement d'écriture
fr.summary: WriteBack-RAG traite la base de connaissances dans la génération augmentée par récupération comme un composant entraînable, utilisant des exemples étiquetés pour identifier et distiller les informations pertinentes en unités de connaissances compactes. L'évaluation sur quatre méthodes RAG et six benchmarks montre des améliorations cohérentes avec une moyenne de +2,14% avec des bénéfices de connaissances se transférant entre différents pipelines RAG.

de.title: Die Wissensdatenbank durch Beweiswärmung und Rückschreib-Anreicherung trainieren
de.summary: WriteBack-RAG behandelt die Wissensdatenbank bei der retrieval-augmentierten Generierung als trainierbare Komponente, die markierte Beispiele verwendet, um relevante Informationen zu identifizieren und in kompakte Wissenseinheiten zu destillieren. Die Bewertung über vier RAG-Methoden und sechs Benchmarks zeigt konsistente Verbesserungen mit durchschnittlich +2,14%, wobei Wissensergebnisse zwischen verschiedenen RAG-Pipelines übertragen werden.

es.title: Entrenamiento de la base de conocimientos mediante destilación de evidencia y enriquecimiento de escritura posterior
es.summary: WriteBack-RAG trata la base de conocimientos en la generación aumentada por recuperación como un componente entrenable, utilizando ejemplos etiquetados para identificar y destilar información relevante en unidades de conocimiento compactas. La evaluación en cuatro métodos RAG y seis benchmarks muestra mejoras consistentes con un promedio de +2,14% con beneficios de conocimiento transfiriéndose entre diferentes canalizaciones RAG.

title: History of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) chatbots: past, present, and future development
summary: This comprehensive review traces the evolution of chatbot technology from early rule-based systems like ELIZA through transformer-based models like ChatGPT. It synthesizes historical milestones in natural language processing and machine learning that have shaped modern conversational AI capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2402.05122v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This research provides an in-depth comprehensive review of the progress of chatbot technology over time, from the initial basic systems relying on rules to today's advanced conversational bots powered by artificial intelligence. Spanning many decades, the paper explores the major milestones, innovations, and paradigm shifts that have driven the evolution of chatbots. Looking back at the very basic statistical model in 1906 via the early chatbots, such as ELIZA and ALICE in the 1960s and 1970s, the study traces key innovations leading to today's advanced conversational agents, such as ChatGPT and Google Bard. The study synthesizes insights from academic literature and industry sources to highlight crucial milestones, including the introduction of Turing tests, influential projects such as CALO, and recent transformer-based models. Tracing the path forward, the paper highlights how natural language processing and machine learning have been integrated into modern chatbots for more sophisticated capabilities. This chronological survey of the chatbot landscape provides a holistic reference to understand the technological and historical factors propelling conversational AI. By synthesizing learnings from this historical analysis, the research offers important context about the developmental trajectory of chatbots and their immense future potential across various field of application which could be the potential take ways for the respective research community and stakeholders.

zh.title: 生成式人工智能(AI)聊天机器人的历史：过去、现在和未来发展
zh.summary: 这份全面的综述追踪了聊天机器人技术的发展过程，从早期的基于规则的系统(如ELIZA)到基于转换器的模型(如ChatGPT)。它综合了自然语言处理和机器学习中的历史里程碑，这些里程碑塑造了现代对话式人工智能的能力。

fr.title: Histoire des chatbots d'intelligence artificielle (IA) générative : développement passé, présent et futur
fr.summary: Cet examen complet retrace l'évolution de la technologie des chatbots des systèmes basés sur des règles précoces comme ELIZA aux modèles basés sur des transformateurs comme ChatGPT. Il synthétise les jalons historiques du traitement du langage naturel et de l'apprentissage automatique qui ont façonné les capacités modernes de l'IA conversationnelle.

de.title: Geschichte der generativen künstlichen Intelligenz (KI)-Chatbots: Vergangenheit, Gegenwart und zukünftige Entwicklung
de.summary: Diese umfassende Übersicht verfolgt die Entwicklung der Chatbot-Technologie von frühen regelgestützten Systemen wie ELIZA bis hin zu transformatorbasierten Modellen wie ChatGPT. Sie synthetisiert historische Meilensteine in der natürlichen Sprachverarbeitung und dem maschinellen Lernen, die die modernen Fähigkeiten der Conversational AI geprägt haben.

es.title: Historia de los chatbots de inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa: desarrollo pasado, presente y futuro
es.summary: Esta revisión integral rastrea la evolución de la tecnología de chatbots desde sistemas tempranos basados en reglas como ELIZA hasta modelos basados en transformadores como ChatGPT. Sintetiza hitos históricos en procesamiento del lenguaje natural y aprendizaje automático que han moldeado las capacidades modernas de IA conversacional.

title: Malicious LLM-Based Conversational AI Makes Users Reveal Personal Information
summary: This randomized controlled trial with 502 participants demonstrates that malicious LLM-based chatbots can effectively extract personal information from users through conversational strategies. Strategies leveraging social aspects of privacy proved most effective while minimizing perceived risk to users.
content:
arXiv:2506.11680v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: LLM-based Conversational AIs (CAIs), also known as GenAI chatbots, like ChatGPT, are increasingly used across various domains, but they pose privacy risks, as users may disclose personal information during their conversations with CAIs. Recent research has demonstrated that LLM-based CAIs could be used for malicious purposes. However, a novel and particularly concerning type of malicious LLM application remains unexplored: an LLM-based CAI that is deliberately designed to extract personal information from users.
  In this paper, we report on the malicious LLM-based CAIs that we created based on system prompts that used different strategies to encourage disclosures of personal information from users. We systematically investigate CAIs' ability to extract personal information from users during conversations by conducting a randomized-controlled trial with 502 participants. We assess the effectiveness of different malicious and benign CAIs to extract personal information from participants, and we analyze participants' perceptions after their interactions with the CAIs. Our findings reveal that malicious CAIs extract significantly more personal information than benign CAIs, with strategies based on the social nature of privacy being the most effective while minimizing perceived risks. This study underscores the privacy threats posed by this novel type of malicious LLM-based CAIs and provides actionable recommendations to guide future research and practice.

zh.title: 恶意的基于LLM的对话式人工智能使用户泄露个人信息
zh.summary: 这项拥有502名参与者的随机对照试验证明了恶意的基于LLM的聊天机器人可以通过对话策略有效地从用户那里提取个人信息。利用隐私社交方面的策略被证实最有效，同时最小化了用户的感知风险。

fr.title: Les systèmes de dialogue basés sur les LLM malveillants obligent les utilisateurs à révéler des informations personnelles
fr.summary: Cet essai randomisé contrôlé avec 502 participants démontre que les chatbots malveillants basés sur les LLM peuvent extraire efficacement les informations personnelles des utilisateurs par le biais de stratégies conversationnelles. Les stratégies exploitant les aspects sociaux de la confidentialité se sont avérées les plus efficaces tout en minimisant le risque perçu pour les utilisateurs.

de.title: Bösartige LLM-basierte Konversations-KI veranlasst Benutzer, persönliche Informationen preiszugeben
de.summary: Diese randomisierte kontrollierte Studie mit 502 Teilnehmern zeigt, dass böswillige LLM-basierte Chatbots persönliche Informationen von Benutzern durch Gesprächsstrategien effektiv extrahieren können. Strategien, die soziale Aspekte der Privatsphäre nutzen, erwiesen sich als am wirksamsten, während sie das wahrgenommene Risiko für Benutzer minimieren.

es.title: La IA conversacional basada en LLM maliciosa hace que los usuarios revelen información personal
es.summary: Este ensayo controlado aleatorio con 502 participantes demuestra que los chatbots basados en LLM maliciosos pueden extraer efectivamente información personal de los usuarios a través de estrategias conversacionales. Las estrategias que aprovechan aspectos sociales de la privacidad resultaron ser más efectivas mientras minimizan el riesgo percibido para los usuarios.

title: Model2Kernel: Model-Aware Symbolic Execution For Safe CUDA Kernels
summary: Model2Kernel is an automated verification system for detecting memory safety bugs in CUDA kernels used for LLM inference through model-aware dynamic analysis and specialized symbolic execution. Testing on production systems discovered 353 previously unknown bugs with only nine false positives, addressing a critical reliability gap.
content:
arXiv:2603.24595v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The widespread adoption of large language models (LLMs) has made GPU-accelerated inference a critical part of modern computing infrastructure. Production inference systems rely on CUDA kernels to implement core transformer operations, yet these kernels are highly susceptible to memory-safety bugs due to model-dependent tensor layouts, intricate memory indexing, and massive thread-level parallelism. Such bugs can corrupt model weights, crash inference services, or even enable adversarial attacks. Existing techniques either depend on unavailable hardware, incur high overhead, or fail to handle kernel inputs with variable lengths, and none can effectively detect CUDA memory bugs in LLM inference systems. This paper presents Model2Kernel, the first practical system for automatically verifying the memory safety of CUDA kernels used in LLM inference. Model2Kernel performs model-aware dynamic analysis to determine how each model invokes kernels and to classify kernel arguments as either fixed by the model architecture or controlled by model users. Using this information, Model2Kernel then applies CUDA-specialized symbolic execution, supported by new abstractions for dynamic tensor memory and thread identifiers, to accurately pinpoint memory bugs in kernels. In the evaluation on CUDA kernels and models from vLLM, Hugging Face, and recent LLM research papers, Model2Kernel discovers 353 previously unknown bugs while producing only nine false positives, demonstrating its effectiveness.

zh.title: Model2Kernel：用于安全CUDA内核的模型感知符号执行
zh.summary: Model2Kernel是一个自动化验证系统，用于通过模型感知的动态分析和专门的符号执行来检测用于LLM推理的CUDA内核中的内存安全错误。在生产系统上的测试发现了353个以前未知的错误，只有9个误报，解决了一个关键的可靠性问题。

fr.title: Model2Kernel : Exécution symbolique consciente du modèle pour les noyaux CUDA sécurisés
fr.summary: Model2Kernel est un système de vérification automatisé pour détecter les bogues de sécurité mémoire dans les noyaux CUDA utilisés pour l'inférence LLM par le biais d'une analyse dynamique consciente du modèle et d'une exécution symbolique spécialisée. Les tests sur les systèmes de production ont découvert 353 bogues précédemment inconnus avec seulement neuf faux positifs, remédiant à une lacune critique en matière de fiabilité.

de.title: Model2Kernel: Modellbewusste symbolische Ausführung für sichere CUDA-Kernel
de.summary: Model2Kernel ist ein automatisiertes Verifizierungssystem zur Erkennung von Speichersicherheitsfehlern in CUDA-Kerneln, die für LLM-Inferenz verwendet werden, durch modellbewusste dynamische Analyse und spezialisierte symbolische Ausführung. Tests auf Produktionssystemen entdeckten 353 bisher unbekannte Fehler mit nur neun falschen Positiven und behobenen einen kritischen Zuverlässigkeitsmangel.

es.title: Model2Kernel: Ejecución simbólica consciente del modelo para núcleos CUDA seguros
es.summary: Model2Kernel es un sistema de verificación automatizado para detectar errores de seguridad de memoria en núcleos CUDA utilizados para inferencia LLM a través de análisis dinámico consciente del modelo y ejecución simbólica especializada. Las pruebas en sistemas de producción descubrieron 353 errores previamente desconocidos con solo nueve falsos positivos, abordando una brecha crítica de confiabilidad.

title: X-OPD: Cross-Modal On-Policy Distillation for Capability Alignment in Speech LLMs
summary: X-OPD proposes a cross-modal on-policy distillation framework to align speech-based LLMs with their text-based counterparts by enabling the speech model to explore its own distribution while receiving token-level feedback from a text teacher. This approach significantly narrows performance gaps in complex tasks while preserving model capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.24596v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: While the shift from cascaded dialogue systems to end-to-end (E2E) speech Large Language Models (LLMs) improves latency and paralinguistic modeling, E2E models often exhibit a significant performance degradation compared to their text-based counterparts. The standard Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) training methods fail to close this gap. To address this, we propose X-OPD, a novel Cross-Modal On-Policy Distillation framework designed to systematically align the capabilities of Speech LLMs to their text-based counterparts. X-OPD enables the Speech LLM to explore its own distribution via on-policy rollouts, where a text-based teacher model evaluates these trajectories and provides token-level feedback, effectively distilling teacher's capabilities into student's multi-modal representations. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that X-OPD significantly narrows the gap in complex tasks while preserving the model's inherent capabilities.

zh.title: X-OPD：语音大语言模型的跨模态在策略蒸馏能力对齐
zh.summary: X-OPD提出了一个跨模态在策略蒸馏框架，通过使语音模型探索其自身分布同时从文本教师获得令牌级反馈，来实现语音大语言模型与基于文本的对应模型的对齐。这种方法显著缩小了复杂任务中的性能差距，同时保留了模型能力。

fr.title: X-OPD : Distillation Cross-Modale En Politique pour l'Alignement des Capacités des Modèles de Langage Parlé
fr.summary: X-OPD propose un cadre de distillation cross-modale en politique pour aligner les modèles de langage basés sur la parole avec leurs homologues basés sur le texte, en permettant au modèle vocal d'explorer sa propre distribution tout en recevant un retour au niveau des tokens d'un enseignant textuel. Cette approche réduit considérablement les écarts de performance dans les tâches complexes tout en préservant les capacités du modèle.

de.title: X-OPD: Cross-Modal On-Policy-Destillation zur Fähigkeitsausrichtung in Speech-LLMs
de.summary: X-OPD schlägt ein Cross-Modal-On-Policy-Distillation-Framework vor, um sprachbasierte LLMs mit ihren textbasierten Entsprechungen auszurichten, indem das Sprachmodell seine eigene Verteilung erforscht und gleichzeitig Token-Feedback von einem Text-Lehrer erhält. Dieser Ansatz schließt Leistungslücken bei komplexen Aufgaben erheblich, während die Modellfähigkeiten erhalten bleiben.

es.title: X-OPD: Destilación Cross-Modal En-Política para la Alineación de Capacidades en LLMs de Voz
es.summary: X-OPD propone un marco de destilación cross-modal en-política para alinear los LLMs basados en voz con sus contrapartes basados en texto, permitiendo que el modelo de voz explore su propia distribución mientras recibe retroalimentación a nivel de tokens de un profesor textual. Este enfoque reduce significativamente las brechas de rendimiento en tareas complejas mientras preserva las capacidades del modelo.

title: A Learnable SIM Paradigm: Fundamentals, Training Techniques, and Applications
summary: This work establishes structural connections between stacked intelligent metasurfaces and artificial neural networks, proposing a learnable SIM architecture for 6G wireless systems. Proposed applications in multi-user signal separation and anti-jamming demonstrate improved spectrum efficiency in a lightweight manner.
content:
arXiv:2603.24599v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Stacked intelligent metasurfaces (SIMs) represent a breakthrough in wireless hardware by comprising multilayer, programmable metasurfaces capable of analog computing in the electromagnetic (EM) wave domain. By examining their architectural analogies, this article reveals a deeper connection between SIMs and artificial neural networks (ANNs). Leveraging this profound structural similarity, this work introduces a learnable SIM architecture and proposes a learnable SIM-based machine learning (ML) paradigm for sixth-generation (6G)-andbeyond systems. Then, we develop two SIM-empowered wireless signal processing schemes to effectively achieve multi-user signal separation and distinguish communication signals from jamming signals. The use cases highlight that the proposed SIM-enabled signal processing system can significantly enhance spectrum utilization efficiency and anti-jamming capability in a lightweight manner and pave the way for ultra-efficient and intelligent wireless infrastructures.

zh.title: 可学习的SIM范式：基础、训练技术和应用
zh.summary: 这项工作建立了堆叠智能超表面与人工神经网络之间的结构联系，为6G无线系统提出了一个可学习的SIM架构。在多用户信号分离和抗干扰方面的应用展示了以轻量级方式改进的频谱效率。

fr.title: Un Paradigme SIM Apprendre : Fondamentaux, Techniques d'Entraînement et Applications
fr.summary: Ce travail établit des connexions structurelles entre les métasurfaces intelligentes empilées et les réseaux de neurones artificiels, proposant une architecture SIM apprendre pour les systèmes sans fil 6G. Les applications proposées en séparation multi-utilisateurs et anti-brouillage démontrent une efficacité spectrale améliorée de manière légère.

de.title: Ein Erlernbares SIM-Paradigma: Grundlagen, Trainingstechniken und Anwendungen
de.summary: Diese Arbeit etabliert strukturelle Verbindungen zwischen gestapelten intelligenten Metaoberflächen und künstlichen neuronalen Netzen und schlägt eine erlernbare SIM-Architektur für 6G-Funksysteme vor. Vorgeschlagene Anwendungen in Multi-Benutzer-Signaltrennung und Jamming-Abwehr demonstrieren eine verbesserte Spektrumeffizienz auf leichte Weise.

es.title: Un Paradigma SIM Aprendible: Fundamentos, Técnicas de Entrenamiento y Aplicaciones
es.summary: Este trabajo establece conexiones estructurales entre metasuperficies inteligentes apiladas y redes neuronales artificiales, proponiendo una arquitectura SIM aprendible para sistemas inalámbricos 6G. Las aplicaciones propuestas en separación de señales multiusuario y anti-jamming demuestran una eficiencia espectral mejorada de manera ligera.

title: FED-HARGPT: A Hybrid Centralized-Federated Approach of a Transformer-based Architecture for Human Context Recognition
summary: This research applies federated learning and transformer architectures to human activity recognition using mobile sensor data from wearables within the Flower framework. Results demonstrate that federated learning achieves comparable accuracy to centralized models while preserving data privacy in non-IID data scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2603.24601v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The study explores a hybrid centralized-federated approach for Human Activity Recognition (HAR) using a Transformer-based architecture. With the increasing ubiquity of edge devices, such as smartphones and wearables, a significant amount of private data from wearable and inertial sensors is generated, facilitating discreet monitoring of human activities, including resting, sleeping, and walking. This research focuses on deploying HAR technologies using mobile sensor data and leveraging Federated Learning within the Flower framework to evaluate the training of a federated model derived from a centralized baseline. The experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed hybrid approach in improving the accuracy and robustness of HAR models while preserving data privacy in a non-IID data scenario. The federated learning setup demonstrated comparable performance to centralized models, highlighting the potential of federated learning to strike a balance between data privacy and model performance in real-world applications.

zh.title: FED-HARGPT：用于人类活动识别的混合集中式-联邦方法的基于变换器的架构
zh.summary: 本研究在Flower框架内将联邦学习和变换器架构应用于使用可穿戴式设备的移动传感器数据进行人类活动识别。结果表明，联邦学习在非独立同分布数据场景中实现了与集中式模型相当的准确性，同时保护了数据隐私。

fr.title: FED-HARGPT : Une Approche Hybride Centralisée-Fédérée d'une Architecture Basée sur Transformateur pour la Reconnaissance du Contexte Humain
fr.summary: Cette recherche applique l'apprentissage fédéré et les architectures de transformateur à la reconnaissance d'activité humaine utilisant les données des capteurs mobiles des appareils portables dans le cadre de Flower. Les résultats démontrent que l'apprentissage fédéré atteint une précision comparable aux modèles centralisés tout en préservant la confidentialité des données dans les scénarios de données non-IID.

de.title: FED-HARGPT: Ein hybrider zentralisierter-föderierter Ansatz einer Transformer-basierten Architektur zur Erkennung menschlicher Kontexte
de.summary: Diese Forschung wendet föderiertes Lernen und Transformer-Architekturen auf die Erkennung menschlicher Aktivitäten anhand von Mobilsensordaten von tragbaren Geräten im Flower-Framework an. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass föderiertes Lernen in nicht-IID-Datenszenarios eine mit zentralisierten Modellen vergleichbare Genauigkeit erreicht und gleichzeitig den Datenschutz wahrt.

es.title: FED-HARGPT: Un Enfoque Híbrido Centralizado-Federado de una Arquitectura Basada en Transformador para el Reconocimiento del Contexto Humano
es.summary: Esta investigación aplica aprendizaje federado y arquitecturas de transformadores al reconocimiento de actividad humana utilizando datos de sensores móviles de dispositivos portátiles dentro del marco de Flower. Los resultados demuestran que el aprendizaje federado logra una precisión comparable a los modelos centralizados mientras preserva la privacidad de los datos en escenarios de datos no-IID.
